verses.text: Blessed <na>(a)[1]</na> is the man, that hath <na>(b)</na> not gone in the counsel of the impious, & hath <na>(c)</na> not stood in the way of sinners, and hath <na>(d)</na> not sit in the chair of pestilence:

But (e) his will is in the way of our Lord, and in his law he will meditate day and night. [1] 1. Hath not gone, not stood, not sit.] ◦ The Hebrew style and manner of discourse differeth here from other nations, in mentioning first the less evil, and the greatest last. Whereas we would say in the contrary order: He is happy that hath not sit, that is, hath not settled himself in wickedness, nor finally persisted obstinate: more happy, that hath not stood, any notable time continued in sin: and most happy, that hath not gone, not given any consent at all to evil suggestions. 2. His will in the way of our Lord.] As one part of happiness consisteth in declining from evil: so the other is in doing good ◦ ; the will desiring, and diligently endeavouring to walk in the way of virtue, and law of God. Which is true justice, and right form of good life, proposed in this Psalm, for attaining eternal beatitude.

And he shall be as a tree, that is planted nigh to <na>(f)</na> the streams of waters, which shall give his fruit in his time. <cr>[1]</cr> And <na>(g)</na> his leaf shall not fall: and <na>(h)</na> all things whatsoever he shall do, shall prosper.
The impious not so, not so: but <na>(i)</na> as dust, which the wind driveth from the face of the earth.
Therefore the impious shall <na>(k)</na> not rise again in judgment: nor sinners in the <na>(l)</na> Council of the just.
For our Lord <na>(m)</na> knoweth the way of the just, and the way of the impious <na>(n)</na> shall perish.

chapters.summary: Christ's glory, the world repining thereat, 4. shall be propagated in all the world. 7. His divine power as well spiritual in converting men's hearts, as external, in severe justice, is prophesied. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: Of Christ, the 5. key. Also of his Church, the 6. key.

verses.text: Why did the <na>(a)</na> Gentiles rage, and <na>(b)</na> peoples meditate vain things? <cr>[1]</cr>
The <na>(c)</na> kings of the earth stood up, and the <na>(d)</na> Princes came together in one against our Lord, and against his Christ.
Let us break their bonds a-sunder: and let us cast away their yoke from us.
He that dwelleth in the heavens shall laugh at them: and our Lord shall scorn them.
Then shall he speak to them in his wrath, & in his fury he shall trouble them.
But I am appointed king by him over Sion his holy hill, preaching his precept.
The Lord said to me: Thou art my Son, I this day have begotten thee.
Ask of me, and I will give thee the Gentiles, for thine inheritance, and thy possession the ends of the earth.
Thou shalt rule them in a rod of iron, and as a potter's vessel thou shalt break them in pieces.
And now ye kings understand: take instruction you that judge the earth.
Serve our Lord in fear: and rejoice to him with trembling.
Apprehend discipline lest sometime our Lord be wrath, and you perish out of the just way.
when his wrath shall burn in short time, blessed are all that trust in him.
The Psalm of David, when he fled from the face of Absalom his son.
Lord <na>(a)</na> why are they <na>(b)</na> multiplied that trouble me? Many rise up against me. <cr>[1]</cr>
Many say to <na>(c)</na> my soul: There is <na>(d)</na> no salvation for him in his God.
But <na>(e)</na> thou Lord art my protector, my <na>(f)</na> glory, & exalting <na>(g)</na> my head.
With my voice I have cried to our Lord: and he hath heard me from his <na>(h)</na> holy hill.
Ihave <na>(i)</na> slept, and have <na>(k)</na> been at rest; and have <na>(l)</na> risen up, because our lord hath taken me.
Iwill not fear thousands of people compassing me: <na>(m)</na> arise Lord, save me my God.
Because thou hast strucken all that are my adversaries without cause: thou hast broken the <na>(n)</na> teeth of sinners.
Salvation <na>(o)</na> is our Lord's: and thy <na>(p)</na> blessing upon thy people.

chapters.summary: King David recounteth his danger, when his son Absalom conspired against him: 4. and thanketh God for his delivery: 9. acknowledging all help to be from God. Mystically, Christ's persecution, Death, Burial, and Resurrection. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: David Persecuted by his son. The 8. key.

verses.text: Unto the end in <na>(a)</na> songs, the Psalm <na>(b)</na> of David.
When <na>(c)</na> I invocated, the God of my justice heard me: in <na>(d)</na> tribulation thou hast enlarged to me. <na>(e)</na> Have mercy on me, and hear my prayer.
Ye sons of men how long are you of heavy heart? Why love you vanity, and seek lying?
And know ye that our Lord hath made his holy one marvelous: our Lord will hear me, when I shall cry to him.
Be ye angry, and sin not: the things that you say in your hearts, in your chambers be ye sorry for.
Sacrifice ye the sacrifice of justice, and hope in our Lord. Many say: Who sheweth us good things?
The light of thy countenance, o Lord is signed upon us: thou hast given gladness in my heart.
By the fruit of their corn, and wine, and oil, they are multiplied.
In peace in the self same I will sleep, and rest:
Because thou Lord hast singularly settled me in hope.

chapters.summary: The holy Prophet teacheth, by his own example, to fly to God in all tribulation: 3. that other refuges are insufficient, 9. and God's help most assured. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: Confidence in God necessary. The 7. key.

verses.text: Unto the end, for <na>(a)</na> her that obtaineth the <na>(b)</na> inheritance. The Psalm of David.
Receive, o Lord <na>(c)</na> my words with thine ears, understand my cry.
Attend to the voice of my prayer, my King and my God.
Because I will pray to thee: Lord in <na>(d)</na> the morning thou wilt hear my voice.
In <na>(e)</na> the morning I will stand by thee and will see: because thou art not a God that wilt iniquity.
Neither shall the malignant <na>(f)</na> dwell near thee: neither shall the unjust abide <na>(g)</na> before thine eyes.
Thou hatest all that work iniquity: thou wilt <na>(h)</na> destroy all that speak lie. The bloody and deceitful man our Lord will abhor:
But I in the multitude of thy mercy. I will enter into thy house: I will adore toward thy holy temple in thy fear.
Lord conduct me in thy justice: because of mine enemies direct my way in thy sight.
Because there is no truth in their mouth: their heart is vain.

Their throat is an open sepulchre, they did deceitfully with their tongues, judge them o God . Let them fail of their cogitations, according to the multitude of their impieties expel them, because they have provoked thee o Lord.
And let all be glad, that hope in thee, they shall rejoice for ever: and thou shalt dwell in them. And all that love thy name shall glory in thee,
because thou wilt bless the just. Lord, as with a shield of thy good will, thou hast crowned us.

chapters.summary: Just men in affliction appeal to God, the revenger of injuries, 5. knowing and professing that God hateth iniquity. 9. Therefore remit their cause to him, 11. recite certain enormous vices of the wicked, 13. and expect God's final judgement of good and bad. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: The general judgement. The 9. key.

verses.text: Unto <na>(a)</na> the end in songs, the Psalm of David for the octave. <cr>[1]</cr>
Lord, <na>(b)</na> rebuke me not in thy fury; nor <na>(c)</na> chastise me in thy wrath.
Have mercy on me Lord, because I am weak: <na>(d)</na> heal me Lord, because all <na>(e)</na> my bones be troubled.
And my soul is <na>(f)</na> troubled exceedingly: but thou Lord <na>(g)</na> how long?
<na>(h)</na> Turn thee, o Lord, and <na>(i)</na> deliver my soul: save me for <na>(k)</na> thy mercy.
Because there is not in <na>(l)</na> death that is mindful of thee: and in <na>(m)</na> hell who shall confess to thee?
Ihave laboured in my sighing, I will every night wash my bed; I will water my couch with my tears.
My eye is troubled for fury: I have waxen old among all mine enemies.
Depart from me all ye that work iniquity: because our Lord hath heard the voice of my weeping.
Our Lord hath heard my petition, our Lord hath received my prayer.
Let all mine enemies be ashamed, & very sore troubled: let them be converted and ashamed very quickly.

chapters.summary: David's earnest and hearty prayer after he had grievously sinned. 5. Which being grounded in filial, not servile fear, 9. concludeth with assured hope, and confidence in God's mercy. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: A pathetical prayer of a sinner & the first penitential Psalm, the 7. key.

verses.text: The Psalm of David which he sung to our Lord, for the words of <na>(a)</na> Chusi the son of Jemini. <cr>[1]</cr>
O Lord my God I have hoped in thee: save me from all that persecute me, and deliver me.
Lest sometime <na>(b)</na> he as a Lion violently take <na>(c)</na> my soul, whilst there is none to redeem, nor to save.
O Lord my God if I have done <na>(d)</na> this, if there be iniquity in my hands:
If I have rendered to them that repayed me evils, let me worthily fall <na>(e)</na> empty from mine enemies.
Let the enemy persecute my soul, and take it, and <na>(f)</na> tread down my life in the earth, and bring down my glory into the dust.
Arise Lord in thy wrath: and <na>(g)</na> be exalted in the coasts of mine enemies.
And arise, o Lord my God, in the <na>(h)</na> precept which thou hast commanded: and a <na>(i)</na> synagogue of peoples shall compass thee.
And <na>(k)</na> for it return on high: our Lord judgeth peoples. Judge me, o Lord, according to <na>(l)</na> my justice, and according to my innocency <na>(m)</na> upon me.
The wickedness of sinners shall be consumed, and thou shalt direct the just, which searchest the heart and reins, o God. <cr>[1]</cr>
My just help is from our Lord, who saveth those that be right of heart.
God is a just judge, strong, & patient: is he angry <na>(n)</na> every day?
<na>(o)</na> Unless you will be converted, he shall shake his sword, he hath bent his bow, and prepared it.
And in it he hath prepared the vessels of death: he hath made his arrows for them that burn. <cr>[1]</cr>
Behold <na>(p)</na> he hath bred with injustice: he hath conceived sorrow, and brought forth iniquity.
He hath opened a pit, and digged it up: and he is fallen into the ditch, which he made.
His <na>(q)</na> sorrow shall be turned upon his head: and his iniquity shall descend upon his crown.
Iwill confess to our Lord according to his justice: and will sing to the name of our Lord most high.

chapters.summary: David in confidence of his just cause, and unjust persecution, prayeth for God's help, 7. and just revenge of his enemies: 15. describing their malicious intention, and ruin. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: David's prayer in tribulation. The 8. key.

verses.text: Unto <na>(a)</na> the end <na>(b)</na> for presses, the Psalm of David.
O Lord <na>(c)</na> our Lord, how marvelous is thy name in the whole earth! Because thy magnificence is elevated above <na>(d)</na> the heavens.
Out of the mouth of infants & sucklings, thou hast perfected praise because of thine enemies, that thou mayest destroy the enemy & revenger.
Because I shall see thy heavens, the works of thy fingers: the moon and the stars, which thou hast founded.
What is man, that thou art mindful of him? Or the son of man, that thou visitest him?
Thou hast minished him a little less than Angels; with glory and honour thou hast crowned him:
And hast appointed him over the works of thy hands.
Thou hast subjected all things under his feet, all sheep and oxen: moreover also the beasts of the field.
The birds of the air, & fishes of the sea; that walk the paths of the sea.
O Lord our Lord, how marvelous is thy name in the whole earth!

chapters.summary: God is magnified & praised for his marvelous work of creatures, 5. but especially of mankind, singularly exalted by the Incarnation of Christ. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: Christ's Incarnation. The 5. key.

verses.text: Unto the end, for the <na>(a)</na> secrets of the son, the Psalm of David.
Iwill <na>(b)</na> confess to thee, o Lord, with all my heart: I will tell all thy marvelous things.
Iwill be <na>(c)</na> glad and <na>(d)</na> rejoice in thee: I will sing to thy name, o most High.
In <na>(e)</na> turning mine enemy backward: they shall be weakened, and perish before my face.
Because thou hast done <na>(f)</na> my judgment and my cause: thou hast sit upon the throne which judgest justice.
Thou hast rebuked the <na>(g)</na> Gentiles, and the impious hath perished: their <na>(h)</na> name thou hast destroyed for ever, and for ever and ever.
The swords of the enemy have failed unto the end: and their cities thou hast destroyed.
Their memory hath perished with a sound: and our Lord abideth for ever. He hath prepared his throne in <na>(i)</na> judgment:
and he will judge the whole world in equity, he will judge the people in justice.
And our Lord is made a refuge for the poor: an helper <na>(k)</na> in opportunities, in tribulation.
And let them hope in thee that know thy name: because thou hast not forsaken them that seek thee, o Lord.
Sing to our Lord, which dwelleth in Sion: declare his <na>(l)</na> studies among the Gentiles.
Because <na>(m)</na> requiring blood he hath remembered them: he hath not forgotten the cry of the poor.
Have mercy on me, o Lord: See my humiliation <na>(n)</na> by my enemies.
Which exaltest me from the gates of death, that I may declare all thy praises in <na>(o)</na> the gates of the daughter of Sion.
Iwill rejoice in thy salvation: the Gentiles are <na>(p)</na> fastened in the destruction, which they made. In this snare, which they hid, is their foot taken.
Our Lord shall be known doing judgments: the sinner is taken in the works of his own hands.
<na>(q)</na> Let sinners be turned into hell, all nations that forget God.
Because to the end there shall not be oblivion of the poor man: the patience of the poor, shall not perish in the end.
Arise Lord, let not man be strengthened: Let the <na>(r)</na> Gentiles be judged in thy sight.
Appoint Lord (s) a Law-giver over them: that the Gentiles may know that they be men. (t) the Hebrews.
<na>(v)</na> Why Lord hast thou departed far off, despisest in opportunities, in tribulation?
Whilst the impious is proud, the poor is <na>(w)</na> set on fire: <na>(x)</na> they are caught in the counsels which they devise.
Because the sinner is praised in the desires of his soul: and the unjust man is blessed.
The sinner hath exasperated our Lord, according to the multitude of his wrath he shall <na>(y)</na> not seek.
There is no God in his sight: his ways are defiled at all time. Thy judgments are taken away from his face: he shall rule over all his enemies.
For he hath said in his heart: I will not be moved from generation unto generation without evil.
Whose mouth is full of cursing, and bitterness, and guile: under his tongue labour and sorrow.
He sitteth in wait with the rich in secret places, to kill the innocent.
His eyes look upon the poor: he lieth in wait in secret, as a lion in his den.
He lieth in wait to take the poor man violently, violently to take the poor man whilst he draweth him. In his snare he will humble himself, and shall fall when he shall have dominion over the poor.
For he hath said in his heart: God hath forgotten, he hath turned away his face not to see for ever.
Arise Lord God, let thy hand be exalted: forget not the poor.
Wherefore hath the impious provoked God? For he hath said in his heart: He will not inquire.
Thou seest, that thou considerest labour & sorrow: that thou mayest deliver them into thy hands. To thee is the poor left: to the orphan thou wilt be an helper.
Break the arm of the sinner and malignant: his sin shall be sought, and shall not be found.
Our Lord shall reign for ever, and for ever and ever: ye Gentiles shall perish from his land.
Our Lord hath heard the desire of the poor: thy ear hath heard the preparation of their heart.
To judge for the pupil and the humble, that man add no more to magnify himself upon the earth.

chapters.summary: The Church praiseth God for her protection, 4. in repelling the enemy's force, 8. in punishing the wicked, and rewarding the just. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: God's providence in protecting the good and permitting evil. The 3. key.

verses.text: Unto the end the psalm of David. I Trust in our Lord, how say ye to my soul: Pass over unto the mountain as a sparrow?
For behold sinners have bent the bow, they have prepared their arrows in the quiver, that they may shoot in the dark, at them that be right of heart.
For they have destroyed the things, which thou didst perfit: but the just what hath he done?
Our Lord is in his holy temple, our Lord his seat is in heaven.
His eyes have respect unto the poor: his eye-lids examine the sons of men.
Our Lord examineth the just and the impious but he that loveth iniquity, hateth his own soul.
He shall rain snares upon sinners: fire and brimstone, and blast of storms the portion of their cup.
Because our Lord is just and hath loved justice: his countenance hath seen equity.

chapters.summary: David's friends advising him to fly from the persecution of Saul, he answereth, that his trust is in God's protection. 2. Though the persecutor be very malicious, 4. yet God will overthrow him, 5. and deliver the just. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: God's provident care of the just. The 3. key.

verses.text: Unto <na>(a)</na> the end for <na>(b)</na> the octave, the <na>(c)</na> Psalm of David.
Save <na>(d)</na> me Lord, because the holy hath failed <na>(e)</na> because verities are diminished from among the children of men.
They have spoken vain things every one to his neighbour, deceitful lips, they have spoken in heart and heart.
Our Lord destroy all deceitful lips, & the tongue that speaketh <na>(f)</na> great things.
Which have said: We will magnify our tongue, our lips are of us, who is our Lord?
For the misery of the needy, and mourning of the poor, now will I arise, saith our Lord: I will put in <na>(g)</na> a salvation, I will do confidently in him.
Words of our Lord be chaste words: silver examined by fire, tried from the earth, purged seven fold. <cr>[1]</cr>
Thou Lord wilt <na>(h)</na> preserve us: and keep us from this generation for ever.
The impious walk round about: according to thy highness thou hast <na>(i)</na> multiplied the children of men.

chapters.summary: The Prophet describeth the paucity of just men, and aboundance of wicked, both at Christ's first coming in flesh, 6. and second in majesty, in the end of the world. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: The state of the Church in the first and last times of Christ. The 6. key.

verses.text: Unto <na>(a)</na> the end, the Psalm of David. How long, o Lord wilt thou forget me unto the end? How long doest thou turn away thy face from me?
How long shall I put <na>(b)</na> counsels in my soul, sorrow in my heart <na>(c)</na> by day?
How long shall mine enemies be exalted over me? Regard and hear me, o Lord my God.
Illuminate mine eyes that I sleep not <na>(d)</na> in death at any time: lest sometime mine enemy say: I have prevailed against him.
They that trouble me, will rejoice if I be moved: but I have hoped in thy mercy.
My heart shall rejoice in thy salvation: I will sing to our Lord, which giveth me <na>(e)</na> good things: and I will sing to the name of our Lord most high.

chapters.summary: After general gross ignorance and impiety in the world 7. Christ shall be incarnate, the Redeemer of mankind. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: Of Christ's Incarnation. The 5. key.

verses.text: Unto the end, a Psalm of David. The <na>(a)</na> fool hath said in his heart: There is no God. They are corrupt, and are become <na>(b)</na> abominable in their studies: there is <na>(c)</na> not that doth good^ no not one. <cr>[1]</cr>
Our Lord hath looked forth from heaven upon the children of men, to see if there be that understandeth, and seeketh after God.

All have declined, they are become unprofitable together: there is not that doth good, no not one. Their throat is an open sepulchre, with their tongues they did deceitfully, the poison of asps under their lips. Whose mouth is full of cursing and bitterness: their feet swift to shed blood. Destruction and infelicity in their ways, and the way of peace they have not known: there is no fear of God before their eyes. * These three verses being not in the Hebrew, nor Greek, yet are in the English, an. 1577. and are three distinct verses in other psalms. 5. 9. & 35.
Shall not all they know that work iniquity, that devour my people as food of bread?
They have not invocated our Lord, there have they trembled for fear, where no fear was.
Because our Lord is in the just generation, you have confounded the counsel of the poor man: because our Lord is his hope.
Who will give from Sion the salvation of Israel? When our Lord shall have turned away the captivity of his people, Jacob shall rejoice, and Israel shall be glad.
The Psalm of David. Lord who shall dwell in <na>(a)</na> thy tabernacle? Or who shall rest in thy holy hill?
He that walketh <na>(b)</na> without spot, and <na>(c)</na> worketh justice.
He that speaketh truth in his <na>(d)</na> heart, that hath not done guile in his tongue. Nor hath done evil to his neighbour, and hath not taken reproach against his neighbour.
The malignant is brought to nothing in his sight: but them that fear our Lord, he glorifieth. He that sweareth to his neighbour, and deceiveth not,
That hath not given his money to usury, and hath not taken gifts upon the innocent, He that doth these things, shall not be moved for ever.

chapters.summary: Christ, by the mouth of David, declareth his future victory and triumph over the world, 9. and death. <na>[1]</na>

verses.text: Of Christ's victory. The 5. key.
The inscription of the title to David himself. Preserve me, o Lord, because I have hoped in thee.
Ihave said to our Lord: Thou art my God, because thou needest not my goods.
To the Saints that are in his land, he hath made all my wills marvelous in them.
Their infirmities were multiplied: afterward they made haste. I will not assemble their conventicles of blood: neither will I be mindful of their names by my lips.
Our Lord the portion of mine inheritance, and of my cup: thou art he, that will restore mine inheritance unto me.
Cords are fallen to me in goodly places: for mine inheritance is goodly unto me.
Iwill bless our Lord, who hath given me understanding: moreover also even till night my veins have rebuked me.
Iforesaw our Lord in my sight always: because he is at my right hand, that I be not moved.
For this thing my heart hath been glad, and my tongue hath rejoiced: moreover also my flesh shall rest in hope.

Because thou wilt not leave my soul in hell: neither wilt give thy holy one to see corruption . Thou hast made the ways of life known to me, thou shalt make me full of joy with thy countenance: delectations on thy right hand, even to the end.
The prayer of David. Hear, o Lord my <na>(a)</na> justice: attend my petition. With thine ears hear my prayer, <na>(b)</na> not in deceitful lips.
From <na>(c)</na> thy countenance let my judgment proceed: let thine eyes see equities.
Thou hast proved <na>(d)</na> my heart, and visited it <na>(e)</na> by night: <na>(f)</na> by fire thou hast examined me, <na>(g)</na> and there is no iniquity found in me.
That my mouth speak not the works of men: for the <na>(h)</na> words of thy lips I have kept the <na>(i)</na> hard ways.
<na>(k)</na> Perfit my passes in thy paths, that my steps be not moved.
Ihave cried, because thou hast heard me, o God: incline thine ear to me, and hear my words.
Make thy mercies marvelous, which savest them that hope in thee.
From them that resist <na>(l)</na> thy right hand keep me, as the apple of the eye. Under the shadow of thy wings protect me:
from the <na>(m)</na> face of the impious, that have afflicted me. Mine enemies have compassed my soul,
they have shut up their fat: their mouth hath spoken pride.
Casting me forth now have they compassed me: they have set their eyes to bend them unto the earth.
They have taken me as a lion ready to the prey: and as a lion's whelp dwelling in hid places.
Arise Lord, prevent him, and supplant him: deliver my soul from the impious, thy sword

from the enemies of thy hand. Lord from a few out of the land divide them, in their life: their belly is filled of thy secrets. They are filled with children: and they have left their remnants to their little ones.
But I in justice shall appear to thy sight: I shall be filled when thy glory shall appear?

chapters.summary: A just man's prayer in tribulation, 10. describing his enemies' cruelty, 13. by way of imprecation foresheweth their destruction, 15. and declareth that the just shall be satisfied in glory. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: God's providence protecting the just. The 3. key.

verses.text: Unto <na>(a)</na> the end, <na>(b)</na> to the servant of our Lord David, who spake to our Lord the words of this canticle, in the day, that our Lord delivered him out of the hand of all his enemies, and out of the hand of <na>(c)</na> Saul, and he said: <cr>[1]</cr> I will <na>(d)</na> love thee o Lord <na>(e)</na> my strength:
Our Lord is my firmament, and my refuge, and my deliverer.
My God is my helper, and I will hope in him. My protector and the <na>(f)</na> horn of my salvation, and my receiver. <cr>[1]</cr>
Praising I will invocate our Lord: & I shall be saved from mine enemies.
The <na>(g)</na> sorrows of <na>(h)</na> death have compassed me: and <na>(i)</na> torrents of iniquity have troubled me.
The sorrows of <na>(k)</na> hell have compassed me: the <na>(l)</na> snares of death have prevented me.
In my tribulation I have invocated our Lord, and have <na>(m)</na> cried to my God: And <na>(n)</na> he hath heard my voice from his holy temple: and my cry in his sight, hath entered into his ears.
The earth was shaken & trembled: the foundations of mountains were troubled, and were moved, <na>(o)</na> because he was wrath with them.
<na>(p)</na> Smoke arose in his wrath: and fire flamed up from his face: coals were kindled from him.
He <na>(q)</na> bowed the heavens, and descended: and <na>(r)</na> darkness under his feet.
And he <na>(s)</na> ascended upon the cherubs, and flew: he flew upon the wings of winds.
And he put darkness his <na>(t)</na> covert, his tabernacle is round about him: darksome water in the clouds of the air.
Because of the <na>(v)</na> brightness in his sight the clouds passed, hail and coals of fire.
And our Lord thundered from heaven, and the Highest gave his voice: hail and coals of fire.
And he shot his arrows, and dissipated them: he multiplied lightnings, and troubled them.
And the fountains of waters appeared, and the foundations of the world were revealed. At thy rebuke, o Lord, at the blast of the spirit of thy wrath.
He sent from on high, and took me: and he received me out of many <na>(w)</na> waters.
He delivered me <na>(x)</na> from my most strong enemies, and from them that hated me: because they were made strong over me.

They prevented me in the day of mine affliction: and our Lord was made my protector. * So in the rest of this Psalm the Prophet speaketh for most part in proper terms, without Metaphors or other figures. Yet in the mystical sense of Christ, and Christians.
And he brought me out into largeness: he saved me, because he <na>(y)</na> would me.
And our Lord will reward me according to my justice, and according to the purity of my hands he will reward me.
Because I have kept the ways of our Lord, neither have I done impiously from my God.
Because all his judgments are in my sight: and his justices I have not repelled from me.
And I shall be immaculate <na>(z)</na> with him, and shall keep me from mine iniquity.
<na>(z)</na> And our Lord will reward me according to my justice: & according to the purity of my hands in the sight of his eyes.
With the holy thou shalt be holy, and with the innocent man thou shalt be innocent.
And with the elect thou shalt be elect: and with the perverse thou shalt be perverted.
Because thou wilt save the humble people: and the eyes of the proud thou wilt humble.
Because thou dost illuminate my lamp o Lord: my God illuminate my darkness.

chapters.summary: King David's thanks to God for his often delivery from great dangers. Unto the end, the Psalm of David.
The heavens shew forth the glory of God, and the firmament declareth the works of his hands.
Day unto day uttereth word: and night unto night sheweth knowledge.
There are no languages, nor speeches, whose voices are not heard.
Their sound hath gone forth into all the earth; and unto the ends of the round world the words of them.
He put his tabernacle in the sun: & himself as a bridegroom coming forth of his bride-chamber. He hath rejoiced as a giant to run the way,
his coming forth from the top of heaven: And his recourse even to the top thereof: neither is there that can hide himself from his heat.
The law of our Lord is immaculate converting souls: the testimony of our Lord is faithful, giving wisdom to little ones.
The justices of our Lord be right, making hearts joyful: the precept of our Lord lightsome, illuminating the eyes.
The fear of our Lord is holy, permanent for ever and ever: the judgments of our Lord be true, justified in themselves.
To be desired above gold and much precious stone: and more sweet above honey and the honey-comb.
For thy servant keepeth them, in keeping them is much reward.
Sins who understandeth? From my secret sins cleanse me:
and from other mens spare thy servant. If they shall not have dominion over me, then shall I be immaculate; and shall be cleansed from the greatest sin.
And the words of my mouth shall be such as may please: and the meditation of my heart in thy sight always. O Lord my helper and my Redeemer.

Unto the end for the <na>(a)</na> morning enterprise, the Psalm <na>(b)</na> of David.

<na>(t)</na> He hoped in the Lord, let him deliver him: let him save him, because he <na>(v)</na> will him.

depart not from me. Because tribulation is very nigh: because there is <na>(z)</na> not that will help.
Many <na>(a)</na> calves have compassed me: <na>(b)</na> fat bulls have besieged me.
They have <na>(c)</na> opened their mouth upon me, as a lion ravening and roaring.
As <na>(d)</na> water I am poured out: and all <na>(e)</na> my bones are dispersed. <na>(f)</na> My heart is made as wax melting in the midst of my belly.
My strength is withered as a <na>(g)</na> potsherd, and my tongue <na>(h)</na> cleaved to my jaws: and thou hast <na>(i)</na> brought me down into the dust of death.
Because many <na>(k)</na> dogs have compassed me: the counsel of the malignant hath besieged me. They have digged my hands and my feet:
they have <na>(l)</na> numbered all my bones. But themselves have <na>(m)</na> considered and beheld me:
they have divided my garments among them: and upon my vesture they have cast lot.

The poor shall eat, and shall be filled: and they shall praise our Lord that seek after him: their hearts shall live for ever and ever.

Because the kingdom is our Lord's: and he shall have dominion over the Gentiles.

The generation to come shall be shewed to our Lord: and the heavens shall shew forth his justice to the people that shall be born, whom our Lord hath made. particular circumstances, as the Evangelists have since historically recorded, that it may not unfitly be called, The Passion of Jesus Christ according to David. 3. Thou wilt not hear.] Our B. Saviour seeing his most terrible death imminent, prayed conditionally [1] , if it pleased his heavenly Father, to have the same removed from him, & was not heard, as the Psalmist here prophesieth. The principal reason was, because God of his divine charity had decreed, that mankind should be redeemed by this death of his Son. Christ also himself of his excellent charity, consented here unto, & therefore persisted not in his conditional prayer, but added absolutely & prayed, that not his own will, but his Fathers might be fulfilled. And in this he was heard [2] , to his own more glory and other infinite benefits of innumerable souls, as it followeth, v. 25. When I cried to him he heard me. S. Paul also witnesseth (Heb. 5. v. 7.) that Christ offering prayers and supplications to him that could save him from death, was heard for his reverence: that is, in respect of his inestimable merit in human nature united in person to God. Another cause, why Christ was not delivered from violent death [3] , as many holy persons were, when they cried to God in distress, as S. Augustine sheweth (Epist. 120. c. 11.) was for example to Christians, whom God will have to suffer temporal afflictions, and death, for the glory of life everlasting, according to S. Peter's doctrine, Christ suffered for us, leaving an example that you may follow his steps. [4] 17. They have digged.] Of obstinate malice the Jews have corrupted this place (and God knoweth how many others) in the Hebrew text of some editions ◦ , reading caari, which signifieth as a lion, without all coherence of the sense, for caaru, they digged or pierced; to avoid so plain a prophecy of nailing Christ's hands and feet to the cross. 23. I will declare thy name to my brethren.] Here it is evident that this Psalm is of Christ ◦ , not of David, by S. Paul's allegation (Heb. 2. v. 11. 12.) saying: He that sanctifieth (to wit Christ) disdained not to call the sanctified his brethren. 23. In the midst of the Church I will praise thee.] After Christ's Passion and Resurrection, in the rest of this Psalm, other two principal points of Christian Religion are likewise prophesied: His perpetual visible Church, and the B. Sacrament of his body. The former is here prophesied by way of inviting all the seed of Jacob to glorify God (v. 24.) all the seed of Israel to fear him (v. 25.) to wit, innumerable Christians [1] , the true Israelites, the universal Church in the whole world. As for heretical parts or parcels in the world, such as the Donatists, which going forth from the Catholic Church say: Christ hath lost his great Church, the devil hath taken the whole world from him, and he remaineth only in a part of Africa, they do not praise God (saith S. Augustine) but dishonour God and Christ, as if God were not faithful in his promise [2] , as if Christ were dispossessed of his Kingdom the Catholic Church. Lest any should reply, that Christ is praised though the Church be decayed, or be very small, the Holy Ghost hath prevented such arguments, saying v. 26: His praise is in the great Church. Which could neither be verified in the part of Donatists in Afrike, nor now in the part of Protestants since Luther in Europe. Further S. Augustine explicateth [3] , & urgeth the verses following in this Psalm, against the same blind, deaf, and obstinate Donatists, who did not, or would not see, nor hear, that all the ends of the earth shall remember, and be converted to our Lord. The holy Scripture saith not, the ends of the earth, but all the ends. Well go too (saith this great Doctor) peradventure there is but one verse, thou thoughtest upon something else, thou talkedst with thy brother, when one read this; mark, he repeateth and knocketh upon the deaf: All the families of the Gentiles shall adore in his sight: Yet the heretic is deaf, he heareth not, let one knock again: Because the Kingdom is our Lord's, and he shall have dominion over the Gentiles. Hold these three verses brethren. Thus and more S. Augustine against those that think the true Church

verses.text: A <na>(a)</na> Psalm of Canticle, <na>(b)</na> in the dedication of David's house.
Iwill <na>(c)</na> exalt thee, o Lord <na>(d)</na> because thou hast received me: neither hast <na>(e)</na> delighted mine enemies over me.
O Lord my God I have cried to thee, and thou hast <na>(f)</na> healed me.
Lord thou hast <na>(g)</na> brought forth my soul out of hell: thou hast saved me from them that go down into the lake.
Sing to our Lord <na>(h)</na> ye his Saints: and <na>(i)</na> confess to the memory of his holiness.
Because <na>(k)</na> wrath is in his indignation: <na>(l)</na> and life in his will. At <na>(m)</na> evening shall weeping abide: and in the morning gladness.
And I said in my aboundance: <na>(n)</na> I will not be moved for ever.
O Lord <na>(o)</na> in thy will thou hast given strength to my beauty. Thou hast <na>(p)</na> turned away thy face from me, and I became troubled.
To thee o Lord <na>(q)</na> I will cry: and I will pray to my God.
<na>(r)</na> What profit is in my blood, whilst I descend into corruption? Shall dust confess to thee, or declare thy truth?
Our Lord hath heard, and had mercy on me: our Lord is become my helper.
Thou hast turned my mourning into joy unto me: thou hast cut my sackcloth, and hast compassed me with gladness.
That <na>(s)</na> my glory may sing to thee, and I be not compunct: Lord my God for ever <na>(t)</na> will I confess to thee.

chapters.summary: King David by voice and instrument rendereth thanks to God for his peaceable state in the Kingdom, 5. inviteth others to rejoice in God's benefits, teaching by his own example that God sometimes giveth more comfort, sometimes sheweth his wrath, but all for our good. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: David rendereth thanks for his establishment in his Kingdom. The 8. key.

verses.text: Unto <na>(a)</na> the end, the Psalm of David, for <na>(b)</na> excess of mind.
In thee o Lord <na>(c)</na> have I hoped, <na>(d)</na> let me not be confounded for ever: in thy justice deliver me. <cr>[1]</cr>
Incline thine ear to me, make haste to deliver me. Be unto me for a God protector: and for a house of refuge, that thou mayst save me.
Because thou art my strength, and my refuge: and for thy name thou wilt conduct me, and wilt nourish me.
Thou wilt bring me out of this snare, which they have hid for me: because thou art my protector.
Into <na>(e)</na> thy hands I commend my spirit: thou hast <na>(f)</na> redeemed me, o Lord God of truth. <cr>[1]</cr>
Thou hast hated them that observe vanities, unprofitably. But I have hoped in our Lord:
Ishall rejoice and be joyful in thy mercy. Because thou hast respected my humility, thou hast saved my soul out of necessities.
Neither <na>(g)</na> hast thou shut me up in the hands of the enemy: thou hast set my feet in a large place.
Have mercy upon me, o Lord, because I am in tribulation: mine eye is troubled for wrath, my soul, and my belly:
Because <na>(h)</na> my life is decayed for sorrow: and my years for groanings. My strength is weakened for poverty; and my bones are troubled.
Above all mine enemies I am made a reproach, both to my neighbours exceedingly: and <na>(i)</na> a fear to my acquaintance. They that saw me, fled forth from me:
Iam forgotten, from the heart as one dead. I am made as a vessel destroyed:
because I have heard the reprehension of many that abide round about: In that whilst, they assembled together against me, they consulted to take my soul.
But I have hoped in thee. O Lord: I said, Thou art my God:
my lots are in thy hands. Deliver me out of the hands of my enemies, and from them that persecute me.
<na>(k)</na> Illustrate thy face upon thy servant, <na>(l)</na> save me in thy mercy:
Lord let me not be confounded, because I have invocated thee. Let the impious be ashamed, and brought down into hell:
let the deceitful lips be made mute. Which speak iniquity against the just, <na>(m)</na> in pride and <na>(n)</na> abuse.

How great is the multitude of thy sweetness, o Lord which thou <na>(o)</na> hast hid for them that fear thee. Thou hast perfited it for them that hope in thee, <na>(p)</na> in the sight of the children of men.
Thou <na>(q)</na> shalt hide them in the secret of <na>(r)</na> thy face from the disturbance of men. Thou shall protect them in thy tabernacle from the contradiction of tongues.
Blessed be our Lord: because he hath made his mercy marvelous to me in the fenced city.
But I have said in <na>(s)</na> the excess of my mind: I am cast away from the sight of thine eyes. Therefore thou hast heard the voice of my prayer, whilst I cried to thee.
<na>(t)</na> Love our Lord all ye his Saints: because our Lord will require truth, & will repay them aboundantly that do proudly.
Do ye <na>(v)</na> manfully, and let your <na>(w)</na> heart take courage, all ye that <na>(x)</na> hope in our Lord.

chapters.summary: A prayer of a just man exceedingly afflicted, still confident in God. 11. Describing his many calamities (in respect of his enemies) undeserved, 18. prayeth for his own delivery, and their just punishment. 20. Praiseth and thanketh God for his goodness. 24. exhorteth all others to do the same. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: How to pray in affliction. The 7. key.

verses.text: To <na>(a)</na> David himself understanding. Blessed are they, <na>(b)</na> whose iniquities are forgiven: and whose sins <na>(c)</na> be covered. <cr>[1]</cr>
Blessed is the man, to whom <na>(d)</na> our Lord hath not imputed sin, neither is there <na>(e)</na> guile in his spirit.
Because <na>(f)</na> I held my peace, my bones are <cr>[1]</cr> inveterated, <na>(g)</na> whilst I cried all the day.
Because day & night thy hand is made heavy upon me: I <na>(h)</na> am turned in my anguish, whilst <na>(i)</na> the thorn is fastened.
Ihave made my sin known to thee: and my injustice I have not hid. I said: I will confess against me my injustice to our Lord: and thou hast forgiven the impiety of my sin.
For this shall every holy one pray to thee, in time convenient. But yet in the flood of many waters, they shall not approach to him.
Thou art my refuge from tribulation, which hath compassed me: my exultation, deliver me from them that compass me.
Iwill give thee understanding, and will instruct thee in the way, that thou shalt go: I will fasten mine eyes upon thee.
Do not become as horse and mule, which have no understanding. In bit and bridle bind fast their cheeks that approach not to thee.
Many are the scourges of a sinner, but him that hopeth in our Lord mercy shall compass.
Be joyful in our Lord and rejoice ye just, and glory all ye right of heart.

chapters.summary: Forgiveness of sins is a happy thing: 3. whereto many are brought by affliction giving them understanding, so moving them to confess their sins, 6. pray for remission, 10. not despair, but hope in God's mercy, and so rejoice with sincere heart. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: The second penitential Psalm. The 7. key.

verses.text: The Psalm of David. Rejoice ye just <na>(a)</na> in our Lord; <na>(b)</na> praising becometh the righteous.
Confess ye to our Lord on <na>(c)</na> the harp: on <na>(d)</na> a psalter of ten strings sing to him.
Sing ye to him <na>(e)</na> a new song: sing well to him in jubilation.
Because the <na>(f)</na> word of our Lord is right, and all his works are <na>(g)</na> in faith.
He loveth <na>(h)</na> mercy and judgment: the earth is full of the mercy of our Lord.
By <na>(i)</na> the word of our Lord the heavens are established: and by the spirit of his mouth all the power of them,
Gathering together the waters of the sea, as it were in a bottle: putting the depths <na>(k)</na> in treasures.
Let all the earth fear our Lord: and let all the inhabitants of the world be moved at him.
Because he said, and they were made: he commanded and they were created,
Our Lord <na>(l)</na> dissipateth the counsels of nations: and he reproveth the cogitations of people, and he reproveth the counsels of princes.
But the counsel of our Lord abideth for ever, the cogitations <na>(m)</na> of his heart in generation and generation.
Blessed is the nation, whose God is our Lord: the people <na>(n)</na> whom he hath chosen for his inheritance.
Our <na>(o)</na> Lord hath looked from heaven: he hath seen all the children of men.
From his prepared habitation he hath looked upon all that inhabit the earth
Who <na>(p)</na> made their hearts severally: who understandeth all their works.
The <na>(q)</na> king is not saved by much power: and the giant shall not be saved in the multitude of his strength.
The horse faileth <na>(r)</na> to safety, <na>(s)</na> and in the aboundance of his force he shall not be saved.
Behold the eyes of our Lord be upon them that fear him: and on them, that hope upon his mercy.
That he may deliver their souls from death: and nourish them in famine.
Our soul expecteth our Lord: because he is our helper and protector.
Because in him our heart shall rejoice: and we have trusted in his holy name.
<na>(t)</na> Let thy mercy, o Lord, be made upon us, as we have hoped in thee.

chapters.summary: The Prophet exhorteth to praise God, 4. describing his power, providence, mercy, and wisdom. 16. No salvation but by him: 20. And therefore prayeth for his help. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: The perfection of God's works described. The 2. key.

verses.text: To <cr>[1]</cr> David, when he changed his countenance before <na>(a)</na> Abimelech, and he dismist him, and he went away <cr>[2]</cr>
I <na>(b)</na> will bless our Lord at <na>(c)</na> all time: his praise always in my mouth.
In our Lord <na>(d)</na> my soul shall be praised: let the mild hear, and rejoice.
Magnify ye our Lord with me: and let us exalt his name for ever.
Ihave sought out our Lord, and he hath heard me: and from all my tribulations he hath delivered me.
Come ye to him, and be illuminated: and your faces shall not be confounded.
This <na>(e)</na> poor man hath cried, and our Lord hath heard him: and from all his tribulations he hath saved him.
The <na>(f)</na> Angel of our Lord shall put in himself about them that fear him: and shall deliver them.
Taste ye, and see that our Lord is sweet: blessed is the man, that hopeth in him.
Fear ye our Lord all ye his Saints: because there is no lack to them that fear him.
The rich have wanted, and have been hungry: but they that seek after our Lord shall not be diminished of any good.
Come children, hear me: I will teach you the fear of our Lord.
who is the man that will have life: loveth to see good days?
Stay thy tongue from evil: and thy lips that they speak not guile.
Turn away from evil, and do good: seek after peace and pursue it.
The eyes of our Lord upon the just: and his ears unto their prayers.
But the countenance of our Lord is upon them that do evil things: to destroy their memory out of the earth.
The just have cried, and our Lord hath heard them: and out of all their tribulations he hath delivered them.
Our Lord is nigh to them, that are of a contrite heart: and the humble of spirit he will save.
Many are the tribulations of the just: and out of all these our Lord will deliver them.
Our Lord keepeth all their bones: there shall not one of them be broken.
The death of sinners is very ill: and they that hate the just shall offend.
Our Lord will redeem the souls of his servants: and all that hope in him shall not offend.

chapters.summary: King David by his own example being delivered from danger, exhorteth all men to render thanks for God's benefits: 12. shewing wherein justice consisteth, 16. and God's special providence towards the just. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: God's providence. The 3. key.

verses.text: To <na>(a)</na> David himself. Judge <na>(b)</na> o Lord them that hurt me: overthrow them that impugn me.
Take <na>(c)</na> armour and <na>(d)</na> shield: and rise up to help me.
Bring forth the sword, and <na>(e)</na> shut up against them, that persecute me: say to my soul: I am thy salvation.
Let them <na>(f)</na> be confounded & ashamed, that seek my soul. Let them be turned backward, and be confounded that think evil against me.
Be they made as dust before the face of wind; and the Angel of our Lord straitening them.
Let their way be made darkness and slipperness: and the Angel of our Lord pursuing them.
Because they have hid the destruction of their snare for me without cause: in vain have they upbraided my soul.
Let the snare which he knoweth not, come on him; and the net, which he hath hid, catch him: and let him fall into the very same snare.
But <na>(g)</na> my soul shall rejoice in our Lord: and shall be delighted upon <na>(h)</na> his salvation.
All my bones shall say: Lord, who is like to thee? Delivering the needy from the hand of them that are stronger than he: the needy and poor from them that spoil him.
<na>(i)</na> Unjust witnesses rising up, asked me things that <na>(k)</na> I knew not.
They repayed me evil things for good, <na>(l)</na> sterility to my soul.
But I when they were troublesome to me, <na>(m)</na> did put on cloth of hair. I humbled my soul in fasting: and my prayer shall be turned into my bosom.
As a <na>(n)</na> neighbour, as our brother, so did I please: as mourning and sorrowful so was I humbled.
And they <na>(o)</na> rejoiced against me, and <na>(p)</na> came together: <na>(q)</na> scourges were gathered together upon me, and I was <na>(r)</na> ignorant.
They were dissipated, and not compunct, they tempted me, they scorned me with scorning: they gnashed upon me with their teeth.
Lord <na>(s)</na> when wilt thou regard? <na>(t)</na> Restore thou my soul from their malignity, mine only one from the lions.
Iwill confess to thee in <na>(v)</na> the great Church, in a grave people I will praise thee.
Let them not rejoice over me that are my adversaries unjustly: <na>(w)</na> that hate me without cause, and twinkle with the eyes.
Because they spake indeed <na>(x)</na> peaceably to me: and in the anger of the earth speaking they meant guiles.
And they <na>(y)</na> opened their mouth awide upon me: they said, Well, well, our eyes have seen.
Thou hast seen, o Lord, keep not silence: <na>(z)</na> Lord depart not from me.
Arise and attend to my judgment: my God, and my Lord unto my cause.
Judge me according to thy justice, o Lord my God, and let them not rejoice over me.
Let them not say in their hearts: Well, well, to our soul: neither let them say, We have devoured him.
Let <na>(a)</na> them blush and be ashamed together, that rejoice at my evils. Let them be clothed with confusion and shame, that speak great things upon me.
Let <na>(b)</na> them rejoice and be glad, that will my justice: and let them say always: Our Lord be magnified, that will the peace of his servant.
And my tongue shall meditate thy justice, thy praise all the day.

chapters.summary: David in figure of Christ, prophetically by way of invocating God's help, foresheweth his persecution, and the just revenge upon his persecutors, 9. with praise to God. 13. his charity towards his cruel adversaries, 17. whom nevertheless God punisheth, 20. for pretending peace in words, and in fact persecuting: 23. rendering to all as they deserve. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: Christ's persecution. The 5. key.

verses.text: Unto <na>(a)</na> the end, to <na>(b)</na> the servant of our Lord David himself.
The unjust hath said within himself, that he <na>(c)</na> would sin: there is no fear of God before his eyes.
Because he hath done deceitfully in his sight: <na>(d)</na> that his iniquity may be found unto hatred.
The words of his mouth are iniquity, and guile: he <na>(e)</na> would not understand that he might do well.
He hath meditated iniquity in his bed: he hath set himself on every way not good, and malice he hath not hated.
Lord <na>(f)</na> thy mercy is in heaven: and <na>(g)</na> thy truth even to the clouds.
Thy justice as the hills of God: thy judgments are great depth. Men & <na>(h)</na> beasts thou wilt save, o Lord,
as thou <na>(i)</na> hast multiplied thy mercy, o God. But <na>(k)</na> the children of men <na>(l)</na> shall hope in the covert of thy wings.
They shall be inebriated with the plenty of thy house: and with the torrent of thy pleasure thou shalt make them drink.
Because with thee is the fountain of life: and in thy light we shall see light.
Extend thy mercy to them that know thee, and thy justice to them, that are of a right heart.
Let <na>(m)</na> not the foot of pride come to me: and let not <na>(n)</na> the hand of a sinner move me.
There <na>(o)</na> have they fallen that work iniquity: they were expelled, <na>(p)</na> neither could they stand.

chapters.summary: The Prophet describeth the wicked malice of obstinate sinners. 6. Against which he opposeth God's infinite goodness, 9. with his provident mercy towards the worst, and just reward of the good; 12. praying to escape the dangerous gulf of pride. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: God's providence. The 3. key.

verses.text: A Psalm <na>(a)</na> to David himself. Have <na>(b)</na> no emulation toward the malignant: neither envy them that do iniquity.
Because <na>(c)</na> they shall quickly wither as grass: and as the blossoms of herbs they shall soon fall.
<na>(d)</na> Hope in our Lord and do good: and inhabit the land, and thou <na>(e)</na> shalt be fed in the riches thereof.
Be delighted in our Lord: & he will give thee the petitions of thy heart.
<na>(f)</na> Reveal thy way to our Lord, and hope in him: and he will do it.
And he will <na>(g)</na> bring forth thy justice as light: and thy judgment as midday:
Be subject to our Lord, and pray him. Have no emulation in him, that prospereth in his way: in a man that doth injustices.
Cease from wrath, and leave fury: have not emulation that thou be malignant.
Because they that are malignant, shall be cast out: but they that expect our Lord, the same shall inherit <na>(h)</na> the land.
And yet a little while, and the sinner shall not be: and thou shalt seek his place, and shall not find it.
<cr>[1]</cr> But the meek shall inherit the land, and shall be delighted in multitude of peace.
The sinner shall observe the just: & shall gnash upon him with his teeth.
But our Lord shall scorn him: because he foreseeth that his day shall come.
Sinners have drawn out the sword: they have bent their bow; That they may deceive the poor and needy: that they may murder the right of heart.
Let <na>(i)</na> their own swords enter into their hearts: and let their bow be broken.
Better is a little to the just, above much riches of sinners.
Because the arms of sinners shall be broken in pieces: but our Lord confirmeth the just.
Our Lord knoweth the days of the immaculate: and their inheritance shall be for ever.
They shall not be confounded in the evil time, and in the days of famine they shall be filled:
because the sinners shall perish. But the enemies of our Lord forthwith as they shall be honoured and exalted, vanishing shall vanish as smoke.
The sinner shall borrow, and not pay: but the just is merciful and will give.
Because they that bless him shall inherit the land: but they that curse him shall perish.
With our Lord the step of man shall be directed: and he shall like well of his way.
When <na>(k)</na> he shall fall, he shall not be bruised: because our Lord putteth his hand under.
Ihave been young, for I am old: and I have not seen the just forsaken, <na>(l)</na> nor his seed seeking bread.
All the day he is merciful and lendeth: & his seed shall be in blessing.
<na>(m)</na> Decline from evil, & do good: and <na>(n)</na> inhabit for ever & ever.
Because our Lord loveth judgment, and he will not forsake his saints: they shall be preserved for ever. The unjust shall be punished: and the seed of the impious shall perish.
But the just shall inherit the land: and shall inhabit for ever and ever upon it.
The mouth of the just shall meditate wisdom, and his tongue shall speak judgment. <cr>[1]</cr>
The law of his God in his heart: and his steps shall not be supplanted.
The sinner considereth the just: and seeketh to <na>(o)</na> murder him.
But our Lord will not leave him in his hands: neither will he condemn him, when judgment shall be given of him.
Expect our Lord, and keep his way: and he will exalt thee, that thou mayst inherit the land: when the sinners shall perish^ thou shalt see.
Ihave seen the impious highly exalted, and advance as the cedars of Libanus.
And I passed by, and behold he was not: and I sought him, and his place was not found.
Keep innocency, and see equity: because there are <na>(p)</na> remains for the peaceable man.
But the unjust shall perish together: the remains of the impious shall perish.
But the salvation of the just is <na>(q)</na> of our Lord: and he is their protector in the time of tribulation.
And our Lord will help them, and deliver them: and he will take them away from sinners, and save them, because they have hoped in him.

chapters.summary: An exhortation not to envy nor imitate the evil, who for most part prosper in this world, and are damned eternally: but to fly evil and do good, duly considering that God diversly permitteth and punisheth the wicked, and likewise comforteth and afflicteth the just, all for their good. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: An exhortation to contempt of this world. The 7. key.

verses.text: A Psalm of David, in <na>(a)</na> recordation of the sabbath.
Lord <na>(b)</na> rebuke me not in thy fury: <na>(c)</na> nor chastise me in wrath.
Because <na>(d)</na> thy arrows are fast sticked in me: and thou hast <na>(e)</na> fastened thy hand upon me.
There is <na>(f)</na> no health in my flesh at <na>(g)</na> the face of thy wrath: my bones have no peace at <na>(h)</na> the face of my sins.
Because mine iniquities are gone <na>(i)</na> over my head: and as a <na>(k)</na> heavy burden are become heavy upon me.
My <na>(l)</na> scars are putrefied and corrupted, because of my foolishness.
Iam become miserable, and am made <na>(m)</na> crooked even to the end: I went sorrowful all the day.
Because <na>(n)</na> my loins are filled with illusions: and there is no health in my flesh.
Iam afflicted and am humbled exceedingly: I <na>(o)</na> roared for the groaning of my heart.
Lord <na>(p)</na> before thee is all my desire: and my groaning is not hid from thee.
My heart is troubled, my strength hath forsaken me: and the light of mine eyes, and the same is not with me.
My <na>(q)</na> friends, and my neighbours have approached, & stood against me. And they that were near me, stood far off:
and they did violence which sought my soul. And they that sought me evils <na>(r)</na> spake vanities: and meditated guiles all the day.
But I as <na>(s)</na> one deaf did not hear: and as one dumb not opening his mouth.
And I became as a man not hearing: and not having reproofs in his mouth.
Because <na>(t)</na> in thee, o Lord have I hoped, thou wilt hear me, o Lord my God.
<na>(v)</na> Because I said: Lest sometime mine enemies rejoice over me, and whilst my feet are moved, they spake great things upon me.
Because I <na>(w)</na> am ready for scourges: and my sorrow is in my sight always:
Because <na>(x)</na> I will declare my iniquity: and I will <na>(y)</na> think for my sin.
But mine enemies live, and are confirmed over me: and they are multiplied that hate me unjustly.
They that repay evil things for good, <na>(z)</na> detracted from me: because I followed goodness.
<na>(a)</na> Forsake me not, o Lord my God, depart not from me.
Attend unto my help, o Lord the God of my salvation.

chapters.summary: King David, or any other penitent, earnestly prayeth God to remit his sins, and mitigate the pains which he acknowledgeth himself to have deserved, 12. lamenting the afflictions which he suffereth by such as sometimes were his friends, 14. whose tentations he now resisteth, trusting in God, resigning himself to God's will, confessing his own iniquity, and humbly praying for God's help. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: The third penitential Psalm. The 7. key.

verses.text: Unto <na>(a)</na> the end, to <na>(b)</na> Idithun himself, a canticle of David.
I <na>(c)</na> have said: I will keep my ways, that I offend not in my tongue. I have set a guard to my mouth, when the sinner stood against me.
Iwas dumb and humbled, and kept silence <na>(d)</na> from good things: and <na>(e)</na> my sorrow was renewed.
My <na>(f)</na> heart waxed hot within me: & in my meditation a fire shall burn.
Ihave spoken in my tongue: Lord <na>(g)</na> make mine end known to me. And the number of my days what it is: that I may know what is lacking to me.
Behold thou hast put my days measurable, and my <na>(h)</na> substance is as nothing before thee. Doubtless all things are vanity, every man living.
Surely man passeth as <na>(i)</na> an image, yea and he is troubled in <na>(k)</na> vain. He gathereth treasure, and knoweth not to whom he shall gather them.
And now what is my expectation? Is not our Lord? And my substance is with thee.
From all mine iniquities deliver me: <na>(l)</na> a reproach to the foolish thou hast given me.
Iwas dumb, and opened not my mouth, <na>(m)</na> because thou didst it:
Remove thy scourges from me.
By the strength of thy hand I have fainted in reprehensions, for iniquity thou hast chastised man. And thou hast made his <na>(o)</na> soul pine away as a spider: but vainly is every man troubled.
Hear my prayer, o Lord, and my petition; with thine ears receive my tears. Keep not silence: because I am a <na>(p)</na> stranger with thee, and a pilgrim, as my fathers.
Forgive me, that I may <na>(q)</na> be refreshed before I depart: and <na>(r)</na> shall be no more.

chapters.summary: Christ's faithful members after long expectation congratulate his coming in flesh. 6. He directing his speech to his Father, professeth to perform the

verses.text: Redemption of mankind, and to denounce the same in the whole world: 12. prayeth for his servants, undertaking to satisfy for their sins. [1] [1] Christ's coming and redeeming of mankind. The 5. key.
Unto <na>(a)</na> the end, a Psalm to David himself.
Expecting <na>(b)</na> I expected our Lord, and he hath attended to me.
And he heard my prayers, and brought me out of the lake of misery, and from the mire of dregs. And hath set my feet upon a rock: and hath directed my steps.
And he hath put a new canticle into my mouth: a song to our God. Many shall see, and shall fear: and they shall hope in our Lord.
Blessed is the man, whose hope is in the name of our Lord: and hath not had regard to vanities and false madness.

Thou hast done many thy marvelous things, o Lord my God: & in thy cogitations there is none that may be like to thee. I <na>(c)</na> have declared and have spoken: they <na>(d)</na> multiplied above number.
<na>(e)</na> Sacrifice and oblation thou wouldest not: but <na>(f)</na> ears thou hast perfited to me. <cr>[1]</cr> Holocaust and for sin thou didst not require:
then said I, Behold I come. In the <na>(g)</na> head of the book it is written of me,
that I should do thy will: my God I would, and thy law in the midst of my heart.
Ihave <na>(h)</na> declared thy justice in the great Church, lo I will not stay my lips: Lord thou hast known it.
Thy justice I have not hid in my heart: thy truth and thy salvation I have spoken. I have not hid thy mercy, and thy truth from the <na>(i)</na> great Council.
<na>(k)</na> But thou, o Lord, make not thy commiserations far from me: thy mercy, and thy truth have always received me.

Because evils have compassed me, which have no number: mine iniquities have overtaken me, & I was <na>(l)</na> not able to see. They are multiplied above the hairs of my head: and <na>(m)</na> my heart hath forsaken me.
It may please thee, o Lord, to deliver <na>(n)</na> me: Lord have respect to help me.
Let <na>(o)</na> them be confounded and ashamed together, that seek my soul to take it away. Let them be turned backward, and be ashamed that will me evils.
Let them forthwith receive their confusion, that <na>(p)</na> say to me: Well, well.
Let all <na>(q)</na> that seek thee rejoice and be glad upon thee: and let them that love thy salvation, say always: Our Lord be magnified:
But <na>(r)</na> I am a beggar, and poor: Our Lord is careful to me. Thou art <na>(s)</na> my helper, & my protector: my God be not slack.

Unto <na>(a)</na> the end, a Psalm to David himself. <cr>[1]</cr> This Psalm is also applied by the Church in the office of the sick, whom whosoever assisteth in that case, may hope to have assistance in their own like necessity.
Blessed is the man that understandeth concerning <na>(b)</na> the needy, and the poor: in <na>(c)</na> the evil day our Lord will deliver him.
Our Lord <na>(d)</na> preserve him, and give him life, and make him blessed in the land: and <na>(e)</na> deliver him not unto the will of his enemies.
Our Lord help him <na>(f)</na> upon the bed of his sorrow: thou hast turned all his couch in his infirmity.
Isaid: <na>(g)</na> Lord have mercy on me: heal my soul, because I have sinned to thee.
Mine enemies have spoken evils to me: When shall he die <na>(h)</na> and his name perish?
And if <na>(i)</na> he came in to see, he spake vain things: his heart hath gathered together iniquity to himself. He went forth, and spake together.
All mine enemies whispered against me: they did think evils to me.
They <na>(k)</na> have determined an unjust word against me: <na>(l)</na> shall not he that sleepeth add to rise again?
For <na>(m)</na> the man also of my peace, in whom I hoped: who did eat my breads, hath greatly trodden me under-foot. <cr>[1]</cr>
But thou, o Lord, have mercy upon me, and raise me up again: and I <na>(n)</na> will repay them.
In this I have known that thou wouldest me: because mine enemy shall not rejoice over me.
But me thou hast received <na>(o)</na> because of innocency: and thou hast confirmed me in thy sight for ever.

Blessed be our Lord the God of Israel (p) from the beginning of the world, and for evermore: (q) Be it, be it. Some divide the Psalms into five books, supposing the first book to end here with these words, Be it, be it: not observing that the last Psalm hath not this ending. S. Jerom confuteth this opinion by our Saviour's and S. Peter's naming it the book, not books of Psalms. Luc. 20. v. 42. Act. 1. Moreover if this were the end of one book, then the Psalm following should not be called the 41. Psalm but the first Psalm of the second book.

chapters.summary: The Prophet pronounceth them happy that will believe in Christ, coming in humility and poverty. 5. Christ describeth his own poor afflicted state in this life, by reason he is to satisfy for the sins of the world; the malice of his adversaries, 10. especially of Judas, 11. and by way of prayer, prophesieth his own Resurrection. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: Christ's Passion and Resurrection. The 5. key.

verses.text: Unto the end, understanding to the sons of <na>(a)</na> Core.
Even as the hart <na>(b)</na> desireth after the fountains of waters: <na>(c)</na> so doth my soul desire after thee, o God.
My soul hath thirsted after God <na>(d)</na> the strong <na>(e)</na> living: <na>(f)</na> when shall I come and appear before the face of God?
My <na>(g)</na> tears have been breads unto me day and night: whilst it is said to me daily: <na>(h)</na> Where is thy God?

These things have I remembered, and have poured out my soul in me, because I shall pass into the place <na>(i)</na> of a marvelous tabernacle, even to the house of God. In the voice of exultation, and confession: the sound of one feasting.
Why <na>(k)</na> art thou sorrowful my soul? & why dost thou trouble me? Hope in God, because yet I <na>(l)</na> will confess to him: the salvation of my countenance,
and my God. My soul is troubled toward myself; therefore will I be mindful of thee from the land <na>(m)</na> of Jordan, and Hermoniim from the little mountain.
Depth <na>(n)</na> calleth on depth, in the voice of <na>(o)</na> thy flood-gates. All thy high things, and thy ways have passed over me.
In <na>(p)</na> the day our Lord hath commanded his mercy: and <na>(q)</na> in the night a song of him. With me <na>(r)</na> is prayer to the God of my life:
Iwill say to God: Thou art my defender. Why hast thou forgotten me? And why go I sorrowful, whilst mine enemy afflicteth me?
Whilst my bones are broken, mine enemies that trouble me have upbraided me: Whilst they say to me day by day: Where is thy God?
Why art thou heavy, o my soul? & why dost thou trouble me? <na>(s)</na> Hope in God, because yet I will confess to him: the salvation of my countenance, and my God.

chapters.summary: The fervent desire of the just, 6. much afflicted in this life, 12. and assured hope of eternal joy. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: Eternal glory. The 10. key.

verses.text: A Psalm <na>(a)</na> of David. Judge <na>(b)</na> me, o God, & discern my cause from the nation not holy, from the unjust and deceitful man <na>(c)</na> deliver me.
Because thou art God <na>(d)</na> my strength: <na>(e)</na> why hast thou repelled me? And why go I sorrowful <na>(f)</na> whilst the enemy afflicteth me.
Send forth <na>(g)</na> thy light and thy truth: <na>(h)</na> they have conducted me, and have brought me into thy holy hill, and into thy tabernacles.
And <na>(i)</na> I will go in to the altar of God: to God, which <na>(k)</na> maketh my youth joyful. I will confess to thee <na>(l)</na> on the harp <na>(m)</na> o God <na>(n)</na> my God:

<na>(o)</na> why art thou sorrowful, o my soul? And why dost thou trouble me? <na>(p)</na> Hope in God, because yet <na>(q)</na> will I confess to him: the salvation <na>(r)</na> of my countenance, and <na>(s)</na> my God.

chapters.summary: The just invocateth God's sentence against the deceitful, that seek his spiritual overthrow, 4. acknowledgeth his help, from Almighty God the B. Trinity, in whose vision glory consisteth. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: One God the B. Trinity. The 1. key.

verses.text: Unto <na>(a)</na> the end, for the sons of <na>(b)</na> Core to understanding.
O God we have heard with our ears: our fathers have declared to us. The <na>(c)</na> work that thou hast wrought in their days: & in the days of old.
Thy hand destroyed the nations, and thou didst plant them: thou didst afflict the peoples, and expel them.

For <na>(d)</na> not by their own sword did they possess the land, and their own arm did not save them: But thy right hand, and thine arm, and the illumination of thy countenance: because <na>(e)</na> thou wast pleased in them.
Thou art the same my King and my God: which commandest the salvations of Jacob.
In <na>(f)</na> thee we shall turn out our enemies with <na>(g)</na> the horn, & in thy name we shall contemn them that rise up against us.
For I will not hope in my bow: and my sword will not save me.
For thou hast saved us from them that afflict us: and them that hate us thou hast confounded. <cr>[1]</cr>
In God we shall be praised all the day: and in thy name we will confess for ever.
But <na>(h)</na> now thou hast repelled and confounded us: and thou wilt not go forth, o God, in our hosts.
Thou hast turned us back behind our enemies: and they that hated us spoiled for themselves.
Thou hast given us as sheep that are to be eaten: and thou <na>(i)</na> hast dispersed us among the nations.
Thou hast sold thy people <na>(k)</na> without price: and there was <na>(l)</na> no multitude in the exchanges of them.
Thou hast made us a reproach to our neighbours, a scorn and mocking stock to them that are round about us.
Thou hast made us for a parable to the Gentiles: a wagging of the head among the peoples.
All the day my shame is against me, and the confusion of my face hath covered me.
At the voice of the upbraider, and the reproacher: at the face of the enemy and persecutor.
All these things have come upon us, <na>(m)</na> neither have we forgotten thee: and we have not done wickedly in thy testament.
And our heart hath not revolted backward: & <na>(n)</na> thou hast declined our paths from thy way.
<na>(o)</na> Because thou hast humbled us in the place of affliction, and the shadow of death hath covered us.
If we have forgotten the name of our God, and if we have spread forth our hands to a strange god,
Will not God inquire of these things? For he knoweth the secrets of the heart. Because <na>(p)</na> for thee we are killed all the day: we are esteemed as sheep of slaughter. <cr>[1]</cr>
<na>(q)</na> Arise, why sleepest thou, o Lord? Arise, and expel us not to the end.
Why dost thou turn away thy face, forgettest our poverty and our tribulation?
Because <na>(r)</na> our soul is humbled in the dust: our <na>(s)</na> belly is glued in the earth.
Arise Lord, help us: and redeem us for thy name.

chapters.summary: The Prophet describeth the first calling, and difficult state of the Jewish nation, 6. their prosperity at other times. 10. Again their afflictions in captivity, and persecutions. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: The state of the Jews. The 4. key.

verses.text: Unto <na>(a)</na> the end, for them, <na>(b)</na> that shall be changed, <na>(c)</na> to the sons of Core <na>(d)</na> for understanding, <na>(e)</na> a Canticle for the beloved.
My <na>(f)</na> heart hath uttered <na>(g)</na> a good word: I tell my works <na>(h)</na> to the king. My <na>(i)</na> tongue is the pen of a scribe, that <na>(k)</na> writeth swiftly.
<na>(l)</na> Goodly of beauty above the sons of men, grace is poured abroad in thy lips: therefore hath God blessed thee for ever.
Be <na>(m)</na> girded with thy sword upon thy thigh, o most mighty.

With thy beauty and fairness <na>(n)</na> intend, <na>(o)</na> proceed prosperously, and <na>(p)</na> reign, Because of <na>(q)</na> truth, and <na>(r)</na> mildness, and <na>(s)</na> justice: and thy right hand shall conduct thee marvelously.
Thy <na>(t)</na> sharp arrows, the <na>(v)</na> peoples underneath thee shall fall into the hearts of the king's enemies.
Thy seat o God <na>(w)</na> for ever and ever: a rod of direction the rod of thy Kingdom.
Thou <na>(x)</na> hast loved justice, and hast hated iniquity: therefore God, <na>(y)</na> thy God, hath anointed thee with the oil of gladness <na>(z)</na> above thy fellows.
<na>(a)</na> Myrrh, and <na>(b)</na> Aloes, and <na>(c)</na> Cassia from thy <na>(d)</na> garments, from houses of ivory, out of the which
<na>(e)</na> the daughters of kings have delighted thee in thy honour.

The <na>(f)</na> Queen stood on thy right hand in golden raiment: compassed with <na>(g)</na> variety. <na>(h)</na> Hear daughter, and <na>(i)</na> see, and <na>(k)</na> incline thine ear: and forget thy people, and the house of thy father.
And the King <na>(l)</na> will covet thy beauty: because he is the Lord thy God, and <na>(m)</na> they shall adore him.
And the daughters <na>(n)</na> of Tyre with gifts, all the rich of the people shall beseech thy countenance.
All the glory of that daughter of the King is <na>(o)</na> within, in <na>(p)</na> borders of gold,
clothed round about with varieties. <na>(q)</na> Virgins shall be brought to the King after her: her <na>(r)</na> neighbours shall be brought to thee.
They shall be brought in joy and exultation: they shall be brought into the temple of the King.
<na>(s)</na> For thy fathers there are born sons to thee: thou shalt make them Princes over all the earth.
They shall <na>(t)</na> be mindful of thy name in all generation and generation. Therefore shall <na>(v)</na> peoples confess to thee for ever: and for ever and ever.

chapters.summary: David singularly moved in heart and tongue, 3. prophesieth Christ's excellency, endowing his Church with most worthy dowries. 11. By way of exhortation foreshewing her internal and external beauty: 17. with perpetual succession of Pastors feeding the flock even to the world's end. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: Christ most excellently endowing his Church. The 6. key.

verses.text: Unto <na>(a)</na> the end, to the sons of Core, for <na>(b)</na> the secrets.
Our God is a refuge <na>(c)</na> and strength: an <na>(d)</na> helper in tribulations, which <na>(e)</na> have found us exceedingly.
Therefore will we <na>(f)</na> not fear when <na>(g)</na> the earth shall be troubled: and <na>(h)</na> mountains transported into the heart of the sea.
Their waters have sounded, and were troubled: the mountains were troubled in his strength.
The violence of the river <na>(i)</na> maketh the city of God joyful: the Highest hath sanctified his tabernacle.
God is in the midst thereof, it shall not be moved: God will help it <na>(k)</na> in the morning early.
Nations are troubled, and <na>(l)</na> Kingdoms are inclined: he gave <na>(m)</na> his voice, the earth was moved.
The Lord of hosts is with us: the God of Jacob is our defender.
Come ye, & see the works of our Lord, what wonders he hath put upon the earth:
<na>(n)</na> taking away wars even unto the end of the earth. He shall destroy bow, & break weapons: and shields he shall burn with fire.
<na>(o)</na> Be quiet, and see that I am God: I shall be exalted among the gentiles, and I shall be exalted in the earth.
The Lord of hosts is with us: the God of Jacob is our defender.

chapters.summary: The Church in persecution acknowledgeth God's perpetual defence, 5. making her thereby more glorious, 10. sometimes granting rest (11. God himself checking the persecuters) and ever protecting her. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: The Church prospereth also in persecution. The 6. key.

verses.text: Unto the end, for <na>(a)</na> the sons of Core.
All ye Nations <na>(b)</na> clap hands: make jubilation to God in the voice of exultation.
Because our Lord is high, <na>(c)</na> terrible: a great King over <na>(d)</na> all the earth.
He hath made peoples subject <na>(e)</na> to us: & gentiles under our feet.
He hath chosen his inheritance in us: the beauty of Jacob which he loved.
<na>(f)</na> God is ascended in <na>(g)</na> jubilation: and our Lord in the voice of trumpet.
Sing ye to our <na>(h)</na> God, sing ye: Sing ye to our <na>(i)</na> King, sing ye.
Because God is King of all the earth: sing ye <na>(k)</na> wisely.
God shall reign over the gentiles: God sitteth upon his holy seat.
Princes of peoples are gathered together with <na>(l)</na> the God of Abraham: because the strong <na>(m)</na> gods of the earth are exceedingly advanced.

chapters.summary: Gentiles are called, and invited to praise God for his magnificence: for Christ's Ascension, and power. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: Vocation of Gentiles. The 6. key.

verses.text: A Psalm <na>(a)</na> of Canticle to the sons of Core, the <na>(b)</na> second of the Sabbath.
Great is our Lord, and to be praised exceedingly, in <na>(c)</na> the city of our God, in his holy mount.
Mount Sion is founded with the exultation of <na>(d)</na> the whole earth, <na>(e)</na> the sides of the North, the city of the great King.
God shall be known in <na>(f)</na> the houses thereof, <na>(g)</na> when he shall receive it.
For behold the kings of the earth <na>(h)</na> were gathered together: they assembled in one.
They seeing it so were in admiration, were troubled, were moved:
Trembling took them. Their sorrows <na>(i)</na> as a woman travailing.
In a vehement spirit <na>(k)</na> thou shalt break the ships of Tharsis.
<na>(l)</na> As we have heard, so have we seen in the city of the Lord of hosts, in the city of our God: God hath founded it for ever.
We have received thy mercy, o God, <na>(m)</na> in the midst of thy temple.
According to thy name, o God, so also is thy praise unto the ends of the earth: thy right hand is full of <na>(n)</na> justice.
Let mount Sion be glad, and the daughters of Juda rejoice, because of thy judgments, o Lord.
Compass Sion, and embrace ye her: <na>(o)</na> tell ye in her towers.
Set your hearts <na>(p)</na> on her strength: and <na>(q)</na> distribute ye her houses, that you <na>(r)</na> may declare it in another generation.
Because <na>(s)</na> this is God, our God for ever, and for ever and ever: he <na>(t)</na> shall rule us evermore.
