my soul coveteth and <na>(d)</na> fainteth unto the courts of our Lord. My heart and <na>(e)</na> my flesh have rejoiced toward the living God.

For <na>(f)</na> the sparrow also hath found her an house: and <na>(g)</na> the turtle-dove a nest for herself, where she may lay her young ones. Thine <na>(h)</na> altars, o Lord of hosts: my King, and my God.
Blessed are they that dwell in <na>(i)</na> thy house, o Lord: for ever and ever they shall praise thee.
Blessed is the man, whose help is from thee: he <na>(k)</na> hath disposed ascensions in his heart,
in <na>(l)</na> the vale of tears, in the place which he hath appointed.
For <na>(m)</na> the law-giver shall give <na>(n)</na> blessing, they shall go <na>(o)</na> from virtue into virtue: <na>(p)</na> the God of gods shall be seen in Sion.
Lord God of hosts hear my prayer: receive with thine ear, o God of Jacob.
Behold, o God our protector: and <na>(q)</na> look upon the face of thy Christ.

Because <na>(r)</na> better is one day in thy courts, above thousands. I have chosen to be <na>(s)</na> an abject in the house of my God: rather than to dwell <na>(t)</na> in the tabernacles of sinners.
Because God <na>(v)</na> loveth mercy and truth: our Lord will give <na>(w)</na> grace <na>(x)</na> and glory.
He will not deprive them of good things, that <na>(y)</na> walk in innocency: o Lord of hosts, blessed is the man that hopeth in thee.

chapters.summary: Devout persons fervently desire eternal glory, 6. accounting it in the mean time, a happy state to be in the militant Church, 12. where God first giving grace, will give glory in the triumphant. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: Eternal glory. The 10. key.

verses.text: Unto the end, to the children of Core, a Psalm.
O Lord thou <na>(a)</na> hast blessed thy land: thou <na>(b)</na> hast turned away the captivity of Jacob.
Thou <na>(c)</na> hast forgiven the iniquity of thy people: thou <na>(d)</na> hast covered all their sins.
Thou hast mitigated all thy wrath: thou hast turned away from the wrath of thine indignation.
<na>(e)</na> Convert us, o God our Saviour: and <na>(f)</na> avert thy wrath from us.
Wilt thou be wrath with us for ever? Or wilt thou extend thy wrath from generation unto generation?
O God thou <na>(g)</na> being turned shalt quicken us: and thy people <na>(h)</na> shall rejoice in thee.
Shew us, o Lord, thy mercy and give us thy salvation.
Iwill <na>(i)</na> hear what our Lord God will speak in me: <na>(k)</na> because he will speak peace upon his people. And upon <na>(l)</na> his saints: and upon them, that are converted to the heart.
But yet his salvation is nigh to them that <na>(m)</na> fear him: that glory may inhabit in our land.
<na>(n)</na> Mercy and truth have met each other: <na>(o)</na> justice and peace have kissed.
<na>(p)</na> Truth is risen out of the earth: <na>(q)</na> and justice hath looked down from heaven.
For <na>(r)</na> our Lord certes will give benignity: and <na>(s)</na> our land shall give her fruit.
<na>(t)</na> Justice shall walk before him: and shall set his steps in the way.

chapters.summary: With commemoration of God's former benefits, 5. Christ's Incarnation is prophesied, 9. bringing peace and salvation, 11. mercy and justice concurring together. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: Incarnation of Christ. The 5. key.

verses.text: Aprayer <na>(a)</na> to David himself. Incline thine ear, o Lord, & <na>(b)</na> hear me: <na>(c)</na> because I am needy and poor.
Keep my soul, because <na>(d)</na> I am holy: save thy servant my God, that <na>(e)</na> hopeth in thee.
Have mercy on me, o Lord, because I have cried to thee <na>(f)</na> all the day:
make joyful the soul of thy servant, because to thee, o Lord, have I <na>(g)</na> lifted up my soul.
Because thou, o Lord, art <na>(h)</na> sweet, and <na>(i)</na> mild: and <na>(k)</na> of much mercy to all that invocate thee. <na>(l)</na>
Receive, o Lord, my prayer with thine ears: and attend to the voice of my petition.
In the day of my tribulation I have called to thee: because thou hast heard me.
There is not the like to thee amongst gods, o Lord: and there is not according to thy works.
<na>(m)</na> All nations whatsoever thou hast made, shall <na>(n)</na> come, and shall adore before thee, o Lord: and they shall <na>(o)</na> glorify thy name.
Because thou art great and doing marvelous things: thou only art God.
Conduct me, o Lord, in thy way, and I will walk in thy truth: let my heart rejoice that it may fear thy name.
Iwill <na>(p)</na> confess to thee, o Lord my God, with all my heart, and will glorify thy name for ever.
Because thy mercy is great upon me: & thou hast delivered my soul out of <na>(q)</na> the lower hell.
O God the wicked are risen up upon me, and the synagogue of the mighty have sought my soul: and they have not set thee in their sight.
And thou, o Lord, the God of compassion and merciful, patient, and of much mercy, and <na>(r)</na> true.
Have respect to me and have mercy on me, <na>(s)</na> give thine empire to thy servant: and save the son of thy handmaid.
Make with me <na>(t)</na> a sign unto God, that they may see which hate me, and may be confounded: <na>(v)</na> because thou o Lord hast holpen me.

chapters.summary: The Church of Christ beginning in Jerusalem, 3. is extended to all Nations, 5. glorious, 9. and permanent 7. in holy joy. <na>[1]</na>

verses.text: The Catholic Church glorious. The 6. key.
To the children of Core, a Psalm of Canticle. The foundations thereof in the holy <na>(a)</na> mountains:
our Lord loveth the gates of Sion above all the Tabernacles of Jacob.
Glorious things are said of thee, o city of God.
<na>(b)</na> I will be mindful of <na>(c)</na> Rahab, and Babylon knowing me. Behold <na>(d)</na> the foreigners, and Tyre, & the people of the Aethiopians, these <na>(e)</na> were there.
Shall it not be said of Sion: <na>(f)</na> Man and man, is born in her; and <na>(g)</na> the Highest himself founded her?
Our Lord will declare <na>(h)</na> in scriptures of peoples, and <na>(i)</na> of Princes: of those that have been in her.
The <na>(k)</na> habitation in thee, is as it were of all rejoicing.
A Canticle of a Psalm, to the children of Core, unto the end, for <na>(a)</na> Maheleth <na>(b)</na> to answer, <na>(c)</na> of understanding to Eman the Ezrahite.
O Lord the God of my salvation: <na>(d)</na> in the day have I cried, and in the night before thee.
Let my prayer enter in thy sight: incline thine ear to my petition.
Because my soul is replenished with evils: & <na>(e)</na> my life hath approached to hell.
I <na>(f)</na> am accounted with them that descend into the lake. I am become as a man without help,
<na>(g)</na> free among the dead, as the wounded sleeping in the sepulchres, of whom thou art mindful no more: and they are cast off from thy hand.
They have put me in <na>(h)</na> the lower lake: in the dark places, and in the shadow of death.
Thy <na>(i)</na> fury is confirmed upon me: and all thy waves thou hast brought in upon me.
Thou hast made my familiars far from me: they have put me abomination to themselves. I was delivered and came not forth:
mine eyes languished for poverty. I cried to thee o Lord all the day: I stretched out my hands to thee.
Wilt thou <na>(k)</na> do marvels to the dead: or shall physicians raise to life, and they confess to thee?
Shall any <na>(l)</na> in the sepulchre declare thy mercy, and thy truth in perdition?
Shall thy marvelous works be known <na>(m)</na> in darkness: and thy justice in the land of oblivion.
And I o Lord have cried to thee: and in the morning shall my prayer prevent thee.
Why doest thou, o Lord, reject my prayer: turnest away thy face from me?
Iam poor, and in labours <na>(n)</na> from my youth: and being <na>(o)</na> exalted, humbled, and troubled.
Thy wraths have passed upon me: and thy terrors have troubled me.
They have compassed me as water all the day: they compassed me together.
Thou <na>(p)</na> hast made friend, and neighbour far from me and my familiars because of misery.

chapters.summary: A faithful person sore and long afflicted, lamentably complaineth, praying God, 15. not still to repel him, being left desolate 19. without all consolation of friends. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: A prayer in long affliction. The 7 key.

verses.text: Of understanding <na>(a)</na> to Ethan the Ezrahite.
The mercies of our Lord I will sing for ever. In generation <na>(b)</na> and generation I will shew forth thy truth in my mouth.
Because thou saidst: Mercy shall be built up for ever <na>(c)</na> in the heavens: thy truth shall be prepared in them.
Ihave ordained a testament with mine elect, I have sworn to David my servant:
<na>(d)</na> for ever will I prepare thy seed. And I will build thy seat unto generation and generation.
The heavens shall confess thy marvelous works o Lord: yea and thy truth in the Church of Saints.
For who in the clouds shall be equal to our Lord: shall be like to God among <na>(e)</na> the sons of God?
God who is glorified in the counsel of Saints, great and terrible over all that are round about him.
O Lord God of hosts, who is like to thee? Thou art mighty, o Lord, and thy truth round about thee.
Thou rulest over the power of the sea: and the moving of the waves thereof thou doest mitigate.
Thou <na>(f)</na> humbledst the proud one, as one wounded: in the arm of thy strength thou hast dispersed thine enemies.
The heavens are thine, and the earth is thine, the round earth, and the fullness thereof thou hast founded:
the north and the sea thou hast created. <na>(g)</na> Thabor and Hermon shall rejoice in thy name:
thy arm is with might. Let <na>(h)</na> thy hand be confirmed, and thy <na>(i)</na> right hand exalted:
justice and judgment is the preparation of thy seat. Mercy and truth shall go before thy face:
<na>(k)</na> blessed is the people that knoweth jubilation. Lord they shall walk in the light of thy countenance,
and in thy name they shall rejoice all the day: and in thy justice they shall be exalted.
Because thou art the glory of their strength: and in thy good pleasure shall our <na>(l)</na> horn be exalted.
Because our protection is of our Lord: and of the holy one of Israel our King.
Then didst thou speak in vision to thy Saints, and saidst: <cr>[1]</cr> I have put help on the mighty one: & have exalted an elect one of my people.
Ihave found David my servant: with mine holy oil have I anointed him.
For mine hand shall help him: and mine arm shall strengthen him.
The enemy shall nothing prevail in him: and the son of iniquity shall not add to hurt him.
And I will cut down his enemies before his face: and them that hate him I will put to flight.
And my truth, and my mercy with him: and in my name shall his horn be exalted.
And I will put his hand in the sea: and his right hand in the rivers.
He shall invocate me: Thou art my Father: my God, and the protector of my Salvation.
And I will put him the first-begotten, high above the kings of the earth.
Iwill keep my mercy unto him for ever: and my testament faithful to him.
Iwill put his seed for ever and ever: and his throne as the days of heaven.
But if his children shall forsake my law: and will not walk in my judgments.
If they shall profane my justices: and not keep my commandments:
Iwill visit their iniquities with a rod: and their sins with stripes.
But my mercy I will not take away from him: neither will I hurt in my truth.
Neither will I profane my testament: and the words that proceed from my mouth I will not make frustrate.
Once I have sworn in my holy, if I lie to David:
his seed shall continue for ever. <cr>[1]</cr>
And <na>(m)</na> his throne as the Sun in my sight, and <na>(n)</na> as the Moon perfect for ever: and a faithful witness in heaven.
But thou hast repelled and despised: thou hast deferred thy Christ.
Thou hast overthrown the testament of thy servant: thou hast profaned his sanctuary on the earth.
Thou hast destroyed all the hedges thereof: thou hast made the firmament thereof fear.
All that pass by the way have spoiled him: he is become a reproach to his neighbours.
Thou hast exalted the right hand of them that oppress him: thou hast made all his enemies joyful.
Thou hast turned away the help of his sword: and hast not holpen him in battle.
Thou hast destroyed him from emundation: and his seat thou hast broken down to the ground.
Thou hast lessened the days of his time: thou hast overwhelmed him with confusion.
How long, o Lord, doest thou turn away for ever: shall thy wrath burn as a fire?
Remember what my substance is: for hast thou made all the children of men in vain?
Who is the man that shall live, and shall not see death: shall deliver his soul from the hand of hell?
Where are thine old mercies, o Lord, as thou swarest to David in thy truth?
Be mindful, o Lord, of the reproach of thy servants (which I have held in my bosom) of many nations.
Which thine enemies have reproached, o Lord, which they have reproached the commutation of thy Christ.
Blessed be our Lord for ever: Be it, be it.

chapters.summary: God's mercy and truth, with his great promises to David, 6. his power in the whole world, and just judgements, are the true joy of his servants. 20. Christ's kingdom shall remain for ever: 31. yea many offending yet all shall not perish, 39. but after great affliction, 47. God will respect man's infirmity. 50. his own promise, and the enemies reproaching his servants and himself: 53. who is blessed for ever. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: The Church of Christ never faileth. The 6. key.

verses.text: Aprayer <na>(a)</na> of Moyses the man of God. Lord, thou art made a refuge for us: <na>(b)</na> from generation unto generation.
Before <na>(c)</na> the mountains were made, or the earth and the world formed, <na>(d)</na> from everlasting even unto everlasting thou art God.
<na>(e)</na> Turn not away man into humiliation: thou saidst: Be converted ye children of men.
Because <na>(f)</na> a thousand years before thine eyes, are as yesterday that is past. And as a watch in the night,
things that are counted nothing shall their years be.
In <na>(g)</na> the morning as an herb he shall pass, in <na>(h)</na> the morning he shall flourish, and pass: in the evening he shall fall, be hardened, and withered.
Because we have fainted in thy wrath, and in thy fury we are troubled.
Thou hast put <na>(i)</na> our iniquities in thy sight: our age in the light of thy countenance. <cr>[1]</cr>
Because all our days have failed, and in thy wrath we have failed. Our years shall be considered <na>(k)</na> as a spider:

the days of our years in them are <na>(l)</na> seventy years. And if in strong ones <na>(m)</na> eighty years: and the more of them, labour & sorrow. Because <na>(n)</na> mildness is come upon us: and we shall be chastised.
<na>(o)</na> Who knoweth the power of thy wrath: and for fear
to number thy wrath? So make thy right hand known: and men learned in heart, in wisdom.
Turn, o Lord, how long? And be entreated for thy servants.
<na>(p)</na> We are replenished in the morning with thy mercy: and we have rejoiced, and art delighted all our days.
<na>(q)</na> We have rejoiced for the days wherein thou hast humbled us: the years, wherein we have seen evils.
Look upon <na>(r)</na> thy servants, and upon thy works: and <na>(s)</na> direct their children.
And <na>(t)</na> let the brightness of our Lord God be upon us, and <na>(v)</na> direct thou the works of our hands over us: and <na>(w)</na> the work of our hands do thou direct.

chapters.summary: Under the form of prayer, the Psalmist describeth the shortness of man's life & other calamities: God's strict judgement, 13. but first his comfortable mercy, 16. and perpetual regard of his own work. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: Man rightly created fell by sin into miseries. The 6. key.

verses.text: Praise <na>(a)</na> of a <na>(b)</na> Canticle to David. He <na>(c)</na> that dwelleth in the help of the Highest, shall abide in the protection of the God of heaven.
He shall say to our Lord: Thou art my protector, and my refuge: my God I will hope in him.
Because he hath delivered me from <na>(d)</na> the snare of the hunters, and from <na>(e)</na> the sharp word.
With his shoulders shall he overshadow thee: and under his wings thou shalt hope.
With shield shall his truth compass thee: thou shalt not be afraid <na>(f)</na> of the fear in the night.
Of <na>(g)</na> the arrow flying in the day, <na>(h)</na> of business walking in darkness: <na>(i)</na> of invasion, and the midday devil.
Athousand shall fall on thy side, & ten thousand on thy right hand: but to thee it shall not approach.
But thou shalt consider with thine eyes: and shalt see the retribution of sinners.
Because thou o Lord art my hope: thou hast made the Highest thy refuge.
There shall no evil come to thee: and scourge shall not approach to thy tabernacle.
Because he hath given his Angels charge of thee: that they keep thee in all thy ways.
In their hands they shall bear thee: lest perhaps thou knock thy foot against a stone.
Upon the asp and the basiliscus thou shall walk: & thou shalt tread upon the lion, and the dragon.
Because he hath hoped in me, I will deliver him: I will protect him, because he hath known my name.
He shall cry to me, and I will hear him: with him I am in tribulation: I will deliver him, and will glorify him.
With length of days I will replenish him: and I will shew him my salvation.

chapters.summary: Whosoever faithfully and firmly trusteth in God's providence, is secure from all dangers of secret, subtle, and open enemies. 7. His adversaries shall come to ruin. 11. Angels shall defend him: 13. no kind of serpent, nor beast shall hurt him. God himself assureth him of his protection, and of eternal salvation. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: God's providence. The 3. key.

verses.text: A Psalm <na>(a)</na> of Canticle, <na>(b)</na> in the sabbath day.
It is good <na>(c)</na> to confess to our Lord: and to sing to thy name, o Highest.
To shew forth thy mercy in <na>(d)</na> the morning: and thy truth in <na>(e)</na> the night.
In <na>(f)</na> the instrument of ten strings, in <na>(g)</na> Psalter: with Canticle, on <na>(h)</na> the Harp.
Because thou hast delighted me, o Lord, in thy workmanship: and in the works of thy hands I will rejoice.
How are thy works magnified, o Lord! Thy cogitations are made very profound.
The <na>(i)</na> unwise man will not know; and the <na>(k)</na> fool will not understand these things.
When sinners shall spring up as grass: and all that work iniquity shall appear. That they may perish for ever:
but thou the Highest for ever, o Lord.
Because lo thine enemies, o Lord, because lo thine enemies shall perish: and all that work iniquity shall be dispersed.
And <na>(l)</na> my horn shall be exalted, as the unicorn's: <na>(m)</na> and my old age in plentiful mercy.
And <na>(n)</na> mine eye hath looked upon mine enemies: and the malignant rising up against me, mine ears shall hear.
The just shall flourish as a palm tree: as the cedar of Libanus shall he be multiplied.
They that are planted in <na>(o)</na> the house of our Lord, shall flourish in <na>(p)</na> the courts of the house of our God.
As yet shall they be multiplied in plentiful old age: and they shall be well affected,
that they <na>(q)</na> may shew forth: That the Lord our God is righteous, and there is no iniquity in him.

chapters.summary: God is by all manner of voices and instruments to be praised in his admirable works. 7. which the foolish not doing are punished; 11. and the wise are rewarded. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: The works of God admirable. The 2. key.

verses.text: Praise <na>(a)</na> of Canticle <na>(b)</na> to David himself in <na>(c)</na> the day before the sabbath, <na>(d)</na> when the earth was founded. Our Lord <na>(e)</na> hath reigned, he <na>(f)</na> hath put on beauty: our Lord hath <na>(g)</na> put on strength, and hath girded himself. For he hath established <na>(h)</na> the round world, which <na>(i)</na> shall not be moved.
Thy seat is prepared from that time: thou <na>(k)</na> art from everlasting.
The <na>(l)</na> rivers o Lord have lifted up: the rivers have lifted up their voice. The rivers have lifted up their waves,
<na>(m)</na> above the voices of many waters. The surges of the sea are marvelous, <na>(n)</na> marvelous is our Lord on high.
Thy testimonies are made <na>(o)</na> credible exceedingly: <na>(p)</na> holiness becometh thy house, o Lord <na>(q)</na> for length of days.

chapters.summary: The faithful servant of God assuredly professeth, that all the pride, 5. cruelty, 7. foolish imaginations, and secret thoughts of the wicked, are manifest to God. 12. Acknowledgeth himself happy, that he is better instructed of God: whereas he had otherwise been damned. 20. Sharply reprehendeth those that consider not of God's judgements: concluding that the just shall be glorified, and the wicked damned. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: Eternal salvation and damnation. The 10. key.

verses.text: To <na>(a)</na> David himself, in <na>(b)</na> the fourth of the sabbath. Our Lord God of revenges: the God <na>(c)</na> of revenges hath done <na>(d)</na> freely.
Be <cr>[1]</cr> exalted thou that judgest the earth: render retribution to the proud.
How long shall sinners, o Lord: how long shall sinners glory?
Shall they utter, and speak iniquity? Shall all they speak <na>(e)</na> that work injustice?
Thy <cr>[1]</cr> people, o Lord, they have humbled: and thine inheritance they have vexed.
The widow, and the stranger they have slain: and the pupils they have killed.
And <cr>[1]</cr> they have said: The Lord shall not see, neither shall the God of Jacob understand.
Understand ye foolish in the people: and ye fools be wise at sometime.
He that planted the ear, shall he not hear? Or he that made the eye doth he not consider?
He that chastiseth nations, shall he not rebuke: he that teacheth man knowledge?
Our Lord knoweth <na>(f)</na> the cogitations of men: that they be vain.
Blessed is the man whom thou shalt instruct, o Lord, and shalt teach out of thy law.
That thou mayst <na>(g)</na> give him quietness from the evil days: till a pit be digged for the sinner.
Because our Lord <na>(h)</na> will not reject his people: and his inheritance he will not forsake.
Until justice <na>(i)</na> be turned into judgment: and <na>(k)</na> they who are near it, are all that are right of heart.
Who shall rise for me against the malignant? Or who shall stand with me against them that work iniquity?
But that our Lord hath holpen me: within very little my soul had dwelt in hell.
If I said: <na>(l)</na> My foot is moved: thy mercy, o Lord, did help me.
According to the multitude of my sorrows in my heart: thy consolations have made my soul joyful.
Doth the seat of iniquity cleave to thee: which makest <na>(m)</na> labour in precept?
They will hunt after the soul of the just: and will condemn innocent blood.
And our Lord became my refuge: and my God the help <na>(n)</na> of my hope.
And he <na>(o)</na> will repay them their iniquity: and in
Praise <na>(a)</na> of Canticle, <na>(b)</na> to David himself. Come <cr>[1]</cr> , let us <na>(c)</na> rejoice to our Lord: let us make jubilation to God <na>(d)</na> our saviour.
Let us <na>(e)</na> prevent his face in confession: and <na>(f)</na> in Psalms let us make jubilation to him.
Because our Lord is a great God: and a great King above all gods.
Because in his hand are the ends of the earth: and the heights of the mountains be his.
Because the sea is his, and he made it: and his hands formed the dry land.
Come let us adore, <na>(g)</na> and fall down: and weep before our Lord, that made us.
Because he is the Lord <na>(h)</na> our God, and we the people of his pasture, and the sheep <na>(i)</na> of his hand.
<na>(k)</na> Today if ye shall hear his voice, harden not your hearts.
As in the provocation according to the day <na>(l)</na> of the tentation in the desert: where your fathers tempted me, proved me, and saw my works.
<na>(m)</na> Forty years was I <na>(n)</na> offended with that generation, and said: These always err in heart.
And these have not known my ways: as I sware in my wrath: <na>(o)</na> if they shall enter into my rest.

chapters.summary: An invitation to serve and adore Christ our Lord and Messias, 3. as well for the benefits of creating all things, 7. as for his Incarnation, and not to harden our hearts as the Jews did. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: Christ our Lord and King. The 5. key.

verses.text: A Canticle <na>(a)</na> to David <cr>[1]</cr> himself, <na>(b)</na> when the house was built after the captivity. Sing ye to our Lord <na>(c)</na> a new song: sing to our Lord all the earth.
<na>(d)</na> Sing ye to our Lord, and bless his name: shew forth his salvation from day to day.
Shew forth his glory among the Gentiles, his marvelous works in all peoples.
Because our Lord is great, and exceeding laudable: he is terrible above all gods.
Because all the gods of the Gentiles are <na>(e)</na> devils, but our Lord <na>(f)</na> made the heavens.
Confession and beauty in his sight: holiness and magnificence in his sanctification.
Bring to our Lord ye families of Gentiles, bring ye to our Lord glory and honour:
bring to our Lord glory unto his name. Take up hosts, and enter into his courts:
adore ye our Lord in his holy court. Let all the earth be moved before his face:
Say ye among the Gentiles that our Lord <na>(g)</na> hath reigned. For he hath corrected the round world, which shall not be moved: he will judge peoples in equity.
<na>(h)</na> Let the heavens be glad, and the earth rejoice, the sea be moved, and the fullness thereof:
the fields shall be glad, and all things that are in them. Then shall the trees of the woods rejoice
before the face of our Lord, because he cometh: because he cometh to judge the earth. He <na>(i)</na> will judge the round world in equity, and peoples in his truth.

chapters.summary: All peoples & nations are invited to praise the blessed Trinity, 3. for Christ's Incarnation, and spiritual kingdom in all the world. 10. even senseless creatures acknowledging his majesty, 13. and judicial power. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: Christ's divine power. The 5. key.

verses.text: To <na>(a)</na> this David, <na>(b)</na> when his land was restored again to him. Our Lord hath reigned, let the earth rejoice: let <na>(c)</na> many Islands be glad.
<na>(d)</na> Cloud and mist round about him: justice and judgment <na>(e)</na> the correction of his seat.
Fire shall go before him, and shall inflame his enemies round about.
His lightnings <cr>[1]</cr> shined to the round world, the earth saw: and was moved.
The mountains melted as wax, before the face of our Lord: before the face of our Lord all the earth.
The heavens have shewed forth his justice: and all peoples have seen his glory.
Let them all be confounded, that adore <na>(f)</na> sculptiles: and that glory in their idols. Adore him all ye his Angels:
<na>(g)</na> Sion heard, and was glad. And <na>(h)</na> the daughters of Juda rejoiced, because of thy judgments, o Lord.
Because thou Lord most high over all the earth: thou art exalted exceedingly above all gods.
You that love our Lord, hate ye evil, our Lord keepeth the souls of his saints, out of the hand of the sinner he will deliver them.
Light is risen to the just, and joy to the right of heart.
Be glad ye just in our Lord: and confess ye to the memory <na>(i)</na> of his sanctification.

chapters.summary: All men are again invited joyfully to celebrate the marvelous conquest of Christ in all nations, 4. with harp, voice, and instruments: 8. all creatures acknowledging

verses.text: his coming to judge the world. <cr>[1]</cr> <cr>[1]</cr> The Church in all nations. The 6. key.

A Psalm <na>(a)</na> to David himself. Sing ye to our Lord <na>(b)</na> a new song: because he hath done marvelous things. His right hand hath wrought salvation <na>(c)</na> to himself: and his arm is holy.
Our Lord hath <na>(d)</na> made known his salvation: in the sight of the Gentiles he hath revealed his justice.
He hath remembered his mercy, and his truth to <na>(e)</na> the house of Israel. All the ends of the earth have seen the salvation of our God.
Make ye jubilation to God all the earth: <na>(f)</na> chant, and <na>(g)</na> rejoice, and <na>(h)</na> sing. <cr>[1]</cr>
Sing to our Lord on harp, on harp and voice of Psalm:
on long drawn trumpets, and voice of cornet of horn. Make jubilation in the sight of the King our Lord:
let the sea be moved, and the fullness thereof: the round world, and they that dwell therein.
The rivers shall clap with hand: the mountains together shall rejoice
at the sight of our Lord: <na>(i)</na> because he cometh to judge the earth. <na>(k)</na> He will judge the round earth in justice, and the peoples in equity.
Apsalm to David himself. Our Lord hath reigned, <na>(a)</na> let peoples be angry: he that sitteth upon the Cherubs, let <na>(b)</na> the earth be moved.
Our Lord great in Sion: and high above all peoples.
Let them confess to thy great name: because it is terrible and holy.
And the honour of the King <na>(c)</na> loveth judgment. Thou hast prepared directions: thou hast done judgment and justice <na>(d)</na> in Jacob.
Exalt ye the Lord our God, and adore <na>(e)</na> his foot-stool: because it is holy.
Moyses and Aaron in his <na>(f)</na> Priests: and Samuel among them that invocate his name: They invocated our Lord <na>(g)</na> and he heard them:
in a pillar of a cloud he spake to them. They kept his testimonies, & the precept which he gave them.
O Lord our God thou heardest them: God thou wast propitious to them, and taking vengeance upon all their <na>(h)</na> inventions.
Exalt ye the Lord our God, and adore ye in his holy mount: because the Lord our God is holy.

chapters.summary: Christ reigneth, notwithstanding his enemies' repine, is adored (5. also his foot-stool) 6. whom ancient Prophets did invocate. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: Christ our Messias. The 5. key.

verses.text: A Psalm <na>(a)</na> in confession.
Make ye jubilation to God <na>(b)</na> all the earth: serve ye our Lord in gladness. Enter ye in <na>(c)</na> before his sight, in exultation.
Know ye that our Lord <na>(d)</na> he is God: he made us, & not we ourselves. His people, and the sheep of his pasture:
enter ye into <na>(e)</na> his gates in confession, his courts in hymns, confess ye to him. Praise ye his name:
because our Lord is sweet, his <na>(f)</na> mercy for ever, and <na>(g)</na> his truth even unto generation and generation.

chapters.summary: All are invited to rejoice in God, Creator of all. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: One Creator of all things. The 1. key.

verses.text: A Psalm to David himself. Mercy <na>(a)</na> and judgment I will sing to thee, o Lord: I will sing,
and I <na>(b)</na> shall understand in the immaculate way, <na>(c)</na> when thou shalt come to me. I walked through in the innocency of my heart, in the midst of my house.
Idid not propose before mine eyes any unjust thing: I hated them that do prevarication.
Aperverse heart hath not cleaved to me: the malignant declining from me I knew not.
<na>(d)</na> One secretly detracting from his neighbour, him did I persecute. One of a proud eye, and unsatiable heart, with him I did not eat.
Mine eyes are towards the faithful of the earth that they may sit with me. A man that walketh in the immaculate way <na>(e)</na> he did minister to me.
He that doth proudly shall not dwell in the midst of my house: he that speaketh unjust things, hath <na>(f)</na> not directed in the sight of mine eyes.
In <na>(g)</na> the morning did I kill all the sinners of the earth: that I might destroy <na>(h)</na> out of the city of our Lord all those that work iniquity.

chapters.summary: A sinner in affliction of mind prayeth God to deliver him, 10. desolate of all other help. 13. Conceiveth comfort in God's eternal goodness, and singular mercy, in redeeming mankind, and propagating the Church. 24. Prayeth to be made mature in virtue before he die, that he may live with God: 26. Who only and wholly being immutable, establisheth his servants for ever. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: The fifth penitential Psalm. The 7. key.

verses.text: The prayer of the poor, when he shall be anxious, and shall make his petition before our Lord.
Lord hear my <na>(a)</na> prayer: and let my <na>(b)</na> cry come to thee.
Turn <na>(c)</na> not away thy face from me: in what day soever I am in tribulation, incline thine ear to me. In what day soever I shall invocate thee, hear me speedily.
Because my days have vanished as <na>(d)</na> smoke: and my <na>(e)</na> bones are withered as <na>(f)</na> a dry burnt firebrand.
I <na>(g)</na> am strucken as grass, and my heart is withered: because <na>(h)</na> I have forgotten to eat my bread.
For <na>(i)</na> the voice of my groaning, my <na>(k)</na> bone hath cleaved to my flesh.
Iam become like <na>(l)</na> a pelican of the wilderness: I am become as <na>(m)</na> a night crow in the house.
Ihave watched, and am become as <na>(n)</na> a sparrow solitary in the house-top.
All the day did mine enemies upbraid me: and they <na>(o)</na> that praised me sware against me.
Because I did <na>(p)</na> eat ashes as bread, & mingled <na>(q)</na> my drink with weeping.
At <na>(r)</na> the face of thy wrath and indignation: because <na>(s)</na> lifting me up thou hast thrown me <na>(t)</na> down.
My days have declined <na>(v)</na> as a shadow: and I am withered <na>(w)</na> as grass.
<na>(x)</na> But thou o Lord endurest for ever: and <na>(y)</na> thy memorial in generation and generation.
Thou <na>(z)</na> rising up shalt have mercy on Sion: because it is <na>(a)</na> time to have mercy on it, because <na>(b)</na> the time cometh.
Because <na>(c)</na> the stones thereof have pleased thy servants: and they shall have pity on <na>(d)</na> the earth <na>(e)</na> thereof.
And <na>(f)</na> the Gentiles shall fear thy name o Lord, and all <na>(g)</na> the kings of the earth thy glory.
Because our Lord hath built Sion: and he shall be seen in his glory.
He hath had respect to the prayer <na>(h)</na> of the humble: and he hath not despised their petition.
Let these things be written unto another generation: and the people that shall <na>(i)</na> be created shall praise our Lord.
Because he hath looked forth from his high holy place: our Lord from heaven hath looked upon the earth.
That he might hear the groanings of the fettered: that he might loose the children of them that are slain:
That they may <na>(k)</na> shew forth the name of our Lord in Sion: and his praise in Jerusalem.
In the assembling of the people together in one, and kings to serve our Lord.
He answered him in the way of his strength: Shew me the fewness of my days.
Call me not back <na>(l)</na> in the half of my days: thy years are unto generation and generation.
In the beginning, o Lord, thou didst found the earth: and the heavens are the works of thy hands.
They <na>(m)</na> shall perish, but thou art permanent: and they shall all wax old as a garment. <cr>[1]</cr> And as a vesture thou shalt change them, and they shall be changed:
but thou art the self-same, and thy years shall not fail.
The children of thy servants shall inhabit: and <na>(n)</na> their seed shall be directed for ever.
To <na>(a)</na> David himself. My soul <na>(b)</na> bless thou our Lord: and <na>(c)</na> all things, that are within me, his holy name.
My soul bless thou our Lord: and forget not all his retributions.
Who is <na>(d)</na> propitious to all thine iniquities: who <na>(e)</na> healeth all thine infirmities.
Who <na>(f)</na> redeemeth thy life from deadly falling: who <na>(g)</na> crowneth thee in mercy and commiserations.
Who <na>(h)</na> replenisheth thy desire in good things: thy youth <na>(i)</na> shall be renewed as the eagle's.
Our Lord <na>(k)</na> doth mercies: and <na>(l)</na> judgment to all that suffer wrong.
He made his ways <na>(m)</na> known to Moyses, his wills to the children of Israel.
Our Lord is <na>(n)</na> pitiful, and <na>(o)</na> merciful: <na>(p)</na> long suffering, and very merciful.
He will <na>(q)</na> not be angry always: neither will he threaten for ever.
He hath not done to us according to our sins: neither according to our iniquities hath he rewarded us.
For according to the height of heaven from the earth: hath he strengthened his mercy upon them that fear him.
As far <na>(r)</na> as the East is distant from the West: hath he made our iniquities far from us.
As a father hath compassion of his children, so hath our Lord compassion on them that fear him:
because he hath known our making. He remembered that we are dust:
man, his days are as grass, as the flower of the field, so shall he flourish.
Because the spirit shall pass in him, and he shall not stand: and he shall know his place no more.
But the mercy of our Lord from everlasting, and unto everlasting upon them that fear him. And his justice is upon the children's children, to them that keep his testament.
And are mindful of his commandments to do them.
Our Lord hath prepared his seat in heaven: and his Kingdom shall have dominion over all.
Bless our Lord all ye his Angels: mighty in power, doing his word, that fear the voice of his words.
Bless our Lord all ye his hosts: you his ministers, that do his will.
Bless ye our Lord all his works: in every place of his dominion, my soul bless thou our Lord.

chapters.summary: Thanks to God for private 6. and public benefits. 17. His mercy, justice, and other properties are immutable. 20. Angels and all other creatures are invited to praise him. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: Gratitude for God's benefits. The 7. key.

verses.text: To David himself. My soul bless thou our Lord: o Lord my God thou art magnified exceedingly.
Thou hast put on <na>(a)</na> confession and beauty: being clothed with light as with a garment. Stretching out the heaven as a skin:
which coverest the higher parts thereof <na>(b)</na> with waters. Which <na>(c)</na> makest the cloud for thee to ascend on: which walkest upon the wings of winds.
Which makest spirits thine <na>(d)</na> Angels: and thy <na>(e)</na> ministers a burning fire.
Which hast founded the earth upon <na>(f)</na> the stability thereof: it shall not be inclined for ever and ever.
The <na>(g)</na> depth, <na>(h)</na> as a garment, is his clothing: upon the mountains shall waters stand.
At <na>(i)</na> thy reprehension they shall fly: at the voice of thy thunder they shall fear.
The <na>(k)</na> mountains ascend: and the plain fields descend into the place, which thou hast founded for them.
Thou hast set a bound, which <na>(l)</na> they shall not pass over: neither shall they return to cover the earth.
Which sendest forth fountains in the valleys: between the midst of mountains shall waters pass.
All the beasts of the field shall drink: the wild asses shall <na>(m)</na> expect in their thirst.
Over them shall the fowls of the air inhabit: out of the midst of rocks they shall give forth voices.
Watering the mountains from higher places: of the fruit of thy work shall the earth be filled.
Bringing forth grass for beasts, and herb for the service of men. That thou mayst bring forth <na>(n)</na> bread out of the earth:
& <na>(o)</na> wine may make the heart of man joyful. That he may make the face cheerful with <na>(p)</na> oil: and bread may confirm the heart of man.
The trees of the field shall be filled, and the cedars of Libanus, which he hath planted:
there^ sparrows shall make their nest. The house of the heron is the leader of them:
the high mountains for harts: the rock a refuge for the Irchins.
He made the moon for seasons: the sun knoweth his going down.
Thou didst appoint darkness, and night was made: in it shall all the beasts of the wood pass.
The whelps of lions roaring, to raven, and to seek of God meat for themselves.
The sun is risen, & they are gathered together: and in their couches they shall be placed.
Man shall go forth to his work: and to his working until evening.
How magnified are thy works, o Lord! Thou hast made all things in wisdom: the earth is filled with <na>(q)</na> thy possession.
This great sea, and very large, there are <na>(r)</na> creeping beasts, whereof <na>(s)</na> there is no number. Little beasts with great:
there ships shall pass. This <na>(t)</na> dragon, whom thou madest to <na>(v)</na> delude:
all expect of thee that thou give them meat in season.
Thou giving unto them, they shall gather it: thou opening thy hand, all shall be filled with bounty.
But thou turning away the face, they shall be troubled: thou shalt take away their spirit, and they shall fail, and shall return into their dust.
Thou shalt send forth thy spirit, and they shall be created: and thou shalt renew the face of the earth.
Be the glory of our Lord for ever: our Lord will rejoice in his works.
Who looketh upon the earth, & maketh it to tremble: who toucheth the mountains, and they smoke.
Iwill chant to our Lord in my life: I will sing to my God as long as I am.
Let my speech be acceptable to him: but I will take delight in our Lord.
<na>(w)</na> Let sinners fail from the earth, and the unjust, so that they be not: my soul bless thou our Lord.

chapters.summary: The Psalmist inviteth himself and others to praise God, for his marvelous works in the heavens, 5. the earth, and water, 9. limiting their bonds, producing all things necessary for all living creatures, in convenient seasons. 27. With continual providence of all. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: God's works marvelous. The 2. key.

verses.text: Alleluia. <na>(a)</na> Confess ye to our Lord, and invocate his name: <na>(b)</na> shew forth his works among the Gentiles. <cr>[1]</cr>
Chant to him, and sing to him: tell ye all his marvelous works.
Praise ye him in his holy name: let the heart of them rejoice that seek our Lord.
Seek ye our Lord, and be confirmed: seek <na>(c)</na> his face always.
Remember ye his marvelous works, which he hath done: his wonders, and the judgments of his mouth.
The seed of Abraham his servants: the children of Jacob his elect.
He is the Lord our God: in <na>(d)</na> all the earth are his judgments.
He hath been mindful for ever of his testament; of the word which he commanded <na>(e)</na> unto a thousand generations. <cr>[1]</cr>
Which he disposed to Abraham: and of his oath to Isaac.
And he appointed it to Jacob for a precept: and to Israel for an eternal testament,
saying: To thee will I give the land of Chanaan, the cord of your inheritance.
When they were <na>(f)</na> of small number, very few and sojourners thereof:
And they passed from nation into nation, & from Kingdom to another people.
He left not a man to hurt them: and he rebuked kings for their sake:
Touch not my anointed, and toward my Prophets be not malignant.
And <na>(g)</na> he called a famine upon the land: and he destroyed all the strength of bread. <cr>[1]</cr>
He sent a man before them: Joseph was sold to be a servant.
They humbled his feet in fetters, iron passed through his soul, <cr>[1]</cr>
until his word came. The word of our Lord inflamed him:
the king sent, and loosed him; the Prince of the people, and released him.
He appointed him lord of his house: and Prince of all his possession.
That he might instruct his Princes as himself: and might teach his ancients wisdom.
And Israel entered into Aegypt, and Jacob was a sojourner in the land <na>(h)</na> of Cham. <cr>[1]</cr>
And he increased his people exceedingly: and strengthened them over their enemies. <cr>[1]</cr>
He <na>(i)</na> turned their heart that they hated his people: and to work guile toward his servants.
He sent Moyses his servant: Aaron <na>(k)</na> himself whom he chose. <cr>[1]</cr>
He did put in them the words of his signs, and of his wonders in the Land of Cham.
He sent <na>(l)</na> darkness, and obscured: and did <na>(m)</na> not exasperate his words.
He turned their <na>(n)</na> waters into blood: and killed their fishes.
Their land brought forth <na>(o)</na> frogs in <cr>[1]</cr> the inner chambers of their kings.
He said, and <na>(p)</na> the <cr>[1]</cr> caenomyia came: and the <na>(q)</na> sciniphs in all their coasts.
He made their rains <na>(r)</na> hail: fire burning in their land.
And he struck their vines, and their fig-trees: and he destroyed the wood of their coasts.
He said, & <na>(s)</na> the locust came, & the <na>(t)</na> bruchus whereof there was no number.
And it did eat all the grass in their land: and it did eat all the fruit of their land.
And he struck every <na>(v)</na> first begotten in their land: the first fruits of all their labour.
And he brought them forth with gold and silver, and there was not in their tribes a feeble person.
Aegypt was glad at their departure: because the fear of them lay upon them.
He spread a cloud for their protection, and fire to shine unto them by night. <cr>[1]</cr>
They made petition, and the quail came: and he filled them with the bread of heaven.
He divided the rock, and waters flowed: rivers ran in the dry ground.
Because he was mindful of his holy word, which he had uttered to Abraham his servant.
And he brought forth his people in exultation, and his elect in joy.
And he gave them the countries of the Nations: and they possessed the labours of peoples: <cr>[1]</cr>
That they might keep his justifications, and seek after his law.

chapters.summary: The Israelites are exhorted to sing praises to God, 5. for his marvelous benefits towards Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob. 11. Whose particular family, being then small, went from Chanaan into Aegypt. (17. Whither Joseph by God's providence was carried before) there increased in number, was persecuted, 26. delivered by Moyses and Aaron, working many great miracles, 36. protected, and fed in the desert, 44. and finally possessed Chanaan. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: God's special benefits towards the Jews. The 4. key.

verses.text: Alleluia. Confess ye to our Lord <na>(a)</na> because he is good: because his mercy is for ever. <cr>[1]</cr>
<na>(b)</na> Who shalt speak the powers of our Lord, shall make all his praises to be heard?
<na>(c)</na> Blessed are they, that keep judgment, & <na>(d)</na> do justice at all time.
Remember us, o Lord <na>(e)</na> in the good pleasure of thy people: visit us in thy salvation:
To <na>(f)</na> see in the goodness of thine elect, to rejoice in the joy of thy nation: that thou mayst be praised with thine inheritance.
We have <na>(g)</na> sinned with our fathers: we have <na>(h)</na> dealt unjustly, we have <na>(i)</na> done iniquity.
Our fathers in Aegypt did not understand thy marvelous works: they were not mindful of the multitude of thy mercy. And they provoked thee to wrath going up unto the sea, the Red sea.
And he saved them <na>(k)</na> for his name sake; that he might make his power known. <cr>[1]</cr>
And he rebuked the Red sea, and it was made dry: and he led them in the depths as in a desert.
And he saved them from the hand of them that hated them: and he redeemed them out of the hand of the enemy.
And water overwhelmed those that afflicted them: there did not one of them remain.
And they believed his words: and they sang his praises.
They had <na>(l)</na> quickly done, they forgot his works: and they <na>(m)</na> expected not his counsel.
And they coveted concupiscence in the desert: and tempted God in the place without water. <cr>[1]</cr>
He gave them their petition: and sent saturity into their <na>(n)</na> souls. <cr>[1]</cr>
And they provoked Moyses in the camp: Aaron the <na>(o)</na> holy of our Lord.
The earth was opened, and swallowed Dathan, and overwhelmed the congregation of Abiron.
And a fire flamed up in their synagogue: the flame burnt the sinners.
And they made a calf in Horeb: and they adored <na>(p)</na> the sculptile. <cr>[1]</cr>
And they <na>(q)</na> changed their glory into the similitude of a calf that eateth grass.
They forgat God, which saved them, which did great things in Aegypt,
marvelous things in the land of Cham, terrible things in the Red sea.
And <na>(r)</na> he said to destroy them: if Moyses his elect had not stood in the way before him <cr>[1]</cr> . To turn away his wrath that he should not destroy them:
and they esteemed for naught the land that was to be desired. <cr>[1]</cr> They did not believe his word.
and they murmured in their tabernacles: they heard not the voice of our Lord.
And he lifted up his hand over them: to overthrow them in the desert: <cr>[1]</cr>
And to cast down their seed among the Nations: & to disperse them in the countries.
And they were professed to <na>(s)</na> Beelphegor: and they did eat the sacrifices <na>(t)</na> of the dead. <cr>[1]</cr>
And they provoked him in their inventions: & ruin was multiplied on them.
And Phinees stood, and <na>(v)</na> pacified: and the slaughter ceased.
And it was reputed to him unto justice, in generation and generation even for ever.
And they provoked him at the waters of contradiction: and <na>(w)</na> Moyses was vexed for them: <cr>[1]</cr>
because they exasperated his spirit. And he plainly affirmed in his lips:
they destroyed not the nations, of which our Lord spake to them.
And they were mingled among the nations, and learned their works: <cr>[1]</cr>
and they served their sculptiles: and it became a scandal to them.
And they immolated their sons, and their daughters to devils.
And <na>(x)</na> they shed innocent blood: the blood of their sons and of their daughters, which they sacrificed to the sculptiles of Chanaan. And the land was infected with blood,
& was contaminated in their works: and they did fornicate in their inventions.
And our Lord was wrath with fury upon his people: and he abhorred his inheritance.
And he delivered them into the hands of the nations: and they that hated them had the dominion of them.
And their enemies afflicted them, and they were humbled under their hands:
he did often deliver them. But they exasperated him in their counsel: and they were humbled in their iniquities.
And he saw when they were afflicted: and he heard their prayer.
And he was mindful of his testament: and it repented him according to the multitude of his mercy.
And he gave them into mercies in the sight of all, that had taken them.
Save us, o Lord our God: and gather us out of the Nations. That we may confess to thy holy name: & may glory in thy praise.
Blessed be our Lord the God of Israel from everlasting unto everlasting: and all the people shall say: Be it, be it.

chapters.summary: The Prophet exhorteth the people to render thanks and praise to God, 6. for remitting their manifold sins, in the desert. 34. and in the conquered land: 38. foreshewing like sins to come, God's wrath and punishment for the same. 44. & that he will give grace of repentance to some 47. for which he prayeth, and praiseth God. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: The Israelites often sinned, and were mercifully punished. The 4. key.

verses.text: Alleluia. Confess <na>(a)</na> ye to our Lord, because he is good: because his mercy is for ever. <cr>[1]</cr>
Let them say that are <na>(b)</na> redeemed of our Lord, whom he redeemed out of the hand of the enemy: and out of the countries he gathered them:
From <na>(c)</na> the rising of the sun, and the going down: from the north, and the sea.
They <na>(d)</na> wandered in the wilderness, in a place without water: the way of city for habitation they found not.
Hungry and thirsty: their soul fainted in them.
And <na>(e)</na> they cried to our Lord when they were in tribulation: and he delivered them out of their necessities.
And he conducted them into the right way: to go into a city of habitation.
Let <na>(f)</na> the mercies of our Lord confess to him: and his marvelous works to the children of men.
Because he hath filled the empty soul: and the hungry soul he hath filled with good things.
Them that sat in darkness, and in the shadow of death: bound in neediness, and iron.
Because they <na>(g)</na> exasperated the words of God: and they provoked the counsel of the Highest.
And their heart was humbled in labours: they were weakened, neither was there any to help.
And they cried to our Lord when they were in tribulation: and he delivered them out of their necessities.
And he brought them out of darkness, and the shadow of death: and brake their bonds asunder.
Let the mercies of our Lord confess to him: & his marvelous works to the children of men.
Because he hath destroyed the gates of brass; and the bars of iron he hath broken.
He hath received them out of the way of their iniquity: for they were humbled for their injustices.
Their soul did abhor all meat: & they approached even to the gates of death.
And they cried to our Lord when they were in tribulation: and he delivered them out of their necessities.
He sent his word, and healed them: and delivered them out of their destructions.
Let the mercies of our Lord confess to him: & his marvelous works to the children of men.
And let them sacrifice the sacrifice of praise: & shew forth his works in exultation.
They that go down into the sea in ships, making traffic in the great waters.
They have seen the works of our Lord, and his marvelous things in the depth.
He said, and the blast of the storm stood: and the waves thereof were exalted.
They ascend even to the heavens, and they descend even to the depths: their soul pined away in evils.
They were troubled, and were moved as a drunken man: and all their wisdom was devoured.
And <na>(h)</na> they cried to our Lord when they were in tribulation, and he brought them out of their necessities.
And he turned his storm into calm: & the waves thereof were quiet.
And they rejoiced because they were quiet: and he conducted them into the haven of their will.
Let <na>(i)</na> the mercies of our Lord confess to him: and his marvelous works to the children of men.
And let them exalt him in the Church of the people: and the chair of the ancients let them praise him.
<na>(k)</na> He turned <na>(l)</na> the rivers into a desert: and the issues of waters into dryness.
The <na>(m)</na> fruitful land into a salt ground, for the malice of them that inhabit it.
He turned the desert into pools of waters: and the land without water into issues of waters.
And he placed the hungry there, and they built a city of habitation.
And they sowed fields and planted vineyards: and they made fruit <na>(n)</na> of nativity.
And he blessed them, and they were multiplied exceedingly: and their beasts he lessened not.
And <na>(o)</na> they were made few: and were vexed by the tribulation of evils, and with sorrow.
Contempt was poured out upon Princes: and he made them wander where was no way, and not in the way.
And he <na>(p)</na> did help the poor out of poverty: and made families as sheep.
The just shall see, and shall rejoice: & all iniquity shall stop her mouth.
Who is wise and will keep these things? And will understand the mercies of our Lord?

chapters.summary: Again the Psalmist inviteth all men to render thanks to God for their delivery from dangers, or evils in general: 4. particularly from dangers in journey, 10. in prison or captivity, 17. in sickness spiritual and corporal, 23. in navigation, 33. describing the changeable course of things in this world, 38. especially of men's states; 42. for all which the just will praise God. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: God's perpetual providence towards all men.

verses.text: Acanticle <na>(a)</na> of Psalm, to David himself.
My heart is ready, o God, my heart is ready <cr>[1]</cr> : I will chant and will sing in my glory. <cr>[2]</cr>
Arise my glory, arise psalter and harp: I will arise early.
I <na>(b)</na> will confess to thee in peoples, o Lord: and I will sing to thee <na>(c)</na> in the Nations.
Because thy mercy is great above the heavens: & thy truth even to the clouds.
Be exalted above the heavens o God, and thy glory over all the earth: <cr>[1]</cr>
<cr>[1]</cr> that thy beloved may be delivered. Save with thy right hand, and hear me:
God spake in his holy: I will rejoice, and will divide Sichem, and I will measure the vale of tabernacles.
Galaad is mine, and Manasses is mine: and Ephraim the protection of my head. Juda is my king:
Moab the pot of my hope. Upon Idumea I will extend my shoe: the strangers are made my friends.
Who will conduct me into a fenced city? Who will conduct me into Idumea?
Wilt not thou, o God, which hast repelled us, and wilt not thou go forth, o God, in our hosts?
Give us help out of tribulation: because man's salvation is vain.
In God we shall do strength: and he will bring our enemies to nothing.

chapters.summary: The royal Prophet promiseth, 5. and rendereth praises to God, 7. for his delivery from troubles, and advancement in the kingdom, 13. praying God still to help man's infirmity. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: David singeth praises for benefits received. The 8. key.

verses.text: Unto the end, a Psalm of David.
O <na>(a)</na> God conceal not my praise: because the mouth of the sinner, and the mouth of <na>(b)</na> the deceitful man is open upon me.
They <na>(c)</na> have spoken against me with deceitful tongue, and with words of hatred they have compassed me: and they have impugned me without cause.
For that they should love me, they backbited me: but I prayed.
And they set against me evil things for good: and hatred for my love.
Appoint <na>(d)</na> a sinner over him: and <na>(e)</na> let the devil stand on his right hand.
When he is judged, let him come forth condemned: and let his prayer be turned into sin.
Let his days be made few: and let another take his <na>(f)</na> bishoprick. <cr>[1]</cr>
Let <na>(g)</na> his children be made orphans: and his wife a widow.
Let his children be transported wandering, and let them beg: and let them be cast out of their habitations.
Let the usurer search all his substance: and let strangers spoil his labours.
Let there be none to help him: neither let there be any to have pity on his pupils.
Let his children come to destruction: in <na>(h)</na> one generation let his name be clean put out.
Let the iniquity of his fathers return to memory in the sight of our Lord: and let not the sin of his mother be blotted out.
Let them be before our Lord always, and let the memory of them perish out of the earth:
For that he remembered not to do mercy.
And he persecuted the poor, and needy man, and the compunct in heart to kill him.

And he <na>(i)</na> loved cursing, and it shall come to him: and he would not blessing, and it shall be far from him. And he put on cursing as a garment, and it entered as water into his inner parts, and as oil in his bones.
Be it to him as a garment, wherewith he is covered: and as a girdle, wherewith he is always girded.
This is the work of them that detract from me before our Lord: and that speak evils against my soul.
And thou Lord, Lord, do with me for thy name's sake: because thy mercy is sweet. Deliver me
because I am needy and poor: and <na>(k)</na> my heart is troubled within me.
As a shadow when it declineth, am I taken away: and I am shaken as locusts.
My knees are weakened with fasting: and my flesh is changed by reason of oil.
And I am made a reproach to them: they saw me, and wagged their heads.
Help me, o Lord my God: <na>(l)</na> save me according to thy mercy.
And let them know that this is thy hand: & thou, o Lord, hast done it.
They will curse, and thou shalt bless: let them that rise up against me be confounded: but thy servant shall rejoice.
Let them that detract from me, be clothed with shame: and let them be covered with their confusion, as with a double patched cloak.
Iwill confess to our Lord exceedingly with my mouth: and in the midst of many I will praise him.
Because he hath stood on the right hand of the poor, that he might save my soul from the persecutors.

chapters.summary: Christ (by the mouth of David) requesteth of God to be justly declared innocent, and his enemies punished, 6. particularly describing Judas the traitor's malice, 21. and his own temporal afflictions: 26. prayeth, 30. and praiseth God for his delivery. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: Christ persecuted & his enemies punished. The 5. key.

verses.text: A Psalm of David. Our <na>(a)</na> Lord said <na>(b)</na> to my Lord: Sit on my right hand: <na>(c)</na> till I make thine enemies, the foot-stool of thy feet. <cr>[1]</cr>
Our Lord will send forth the rod of thy strength <na>(d)</na> from Sion: rule thou in the midst of thine enemies.
With thee <na>(e)</na> the beginning in the day <na>(f)</na> of thy strength: in <na>(g)</na> the brightness of holy things: from the womb <na>(h)</na> before the day star I begat thee.
Our Lord sware, and it shall not repent him: Thou art a Priest for ever according to the order of Melchisedech.
Our Lord on thy right hand hath broken kings in the day of his wrath.
He shall judge in nations, he shall fill ruins, he shall crush the heads in the land of many.
Of the torrent in the way he shall drink: therefore shall he exalt the head.

chapters.summary: Christ rising and ascending into heaven sitteth on the right hand of God: 2. beginning in Jerusalem reigneth in the Church of the whole earth: 4. useth the Priesthood of Melchisedech's order to the end of the world. 6. And shall judge the world. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: Christ's exaltation. The 5. key.

verses.text: Alleluia. I will confess to thee, o Lord, with all my heart: in <na>(a)</na> the counsel of the just, and <na>(b)</na> the congregation. <cr>[1]</cr>
The works of our Lord are great: exquisite according <na>(c)</na> to all his wills.
<na>(d)</na> Confession and magnificence his work: and his justice continueth for ever and ever.
He hath made <na>(e)</na> a memory of his marvelous works; a merciful & pitiful Lord:
he hath given <na>(f)</na> meat to them that fear him. He will be mindful for ever <na>(g)</na> of his testament:
<na>(h)</na> the force of his works he will shew forth to his people:
To give them the inheritance of the gentiles: the works of his hands truth and judgment.
All his commandments <na>(i)</na> are faithful: confirmed for ever and ever, made in truth and equity.
He sent <na>(k)</na> redemption to his people: he commanded his testament for ever. Holy and terrible is his name:
<na>(l)</na> the fear of our Lord is the beginning of wisdom. Understanding is good to all that do it: his praise remaineth for ever and ever.

chapters.summary: Praise of God for benefits, 4. especially for the B. Sacrament of the Eucharist, 6. with other graces imparted to the Catholic Church. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: Graces given to the Church. The 6. key.

verses.text: Alleluia. <na>(a)</na> Of the return of Aggeus and Zachary. <cr>[1]</cr> Blessed is the man that feareth our Lord: <na>(b)</na> he shall have great delight in his commandments. <cr>[2]</cr>
<na>(c)</na> His seed shall be mighty in the earth: the generation of the righteous shall be blessed.
Glory and riches in his house: and <na>(d)</na> his justice abideth for ever and ever.
<na>(e)</na> Light is risen up in darkness to the righteous: he is merciful, and pitiful, and just.
Acceptable is the man, that is merciful and lendeth, that <na>(f)</na> shall dispose his words in judgment:
Because he shall not be moved for ever.
The just shall be in eternal memory: he shall not fear at the hearing of evil. His heart is ready to hope in our Lord,
his heart is confirmed: he shall not be moved till he look over his enemies.
He distributed, he gave to the poor: <na>(g)</na> his justice remaineth for ever and ever, <na>(h)</na> his horn shall be exalted in glory. <cr>[1]</cr>
The sinner shall see, and will be angry, he shall gnash his teeth and pine away: the desire of sinners shall perish.

chapters.summary: God is to be praised, who being high, regardeth and provideth for the needy in this world. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: God's providence. The 3. key.

verses.text: Alleluia. Praise our Lord ye <na>(a)</na> children: praise ye the name of our Lord.
Be the name of our Lord blessed, from henceforth now and for ever,
From the rising of the sun unto the going down, the name of our Lord is laudable.
Our Lord is high above all nations, and his glory above the heavens.
Who is as the Lord our God, that dwelleth on high,
and beholdeth the low things <na>(b)</na> in heaven and in earth?
Raising up the needy from the earth, and lifting up the poor out of the dung:
<na>(c)</na> To place him with Princes, with the Princes of his people.
Who maketh the <na>(d)</na> barren woman to dwell in a house, a joyful mother of children.
Alleluia. In the coming forth of Israel out of Aegypt: of the house of Jacob from <na>(a)</na> the barbarous people.
<na>(b)</na> Jewry was made his sanctification, Israel his dominion.
The <na>(c)</na> sea saw, and <na>(d)</na> fled: Jordan <na>(e)</na> was turned backward. <cr>[1]</cr>
<na>(f)</na> The mountains leaped as rams: and the little hills as the lambs of sheep.
<na>(g)</na> What aileth thee o sea that thou didst fly: and thou o Jordan, that thou was turned backward?
Ye mountains leaped as rams, and ye little hills as the lambs of sheep.
At the face of our Lord was the earth moved, at the face of the God of Jacob.
Who turned <na>(h)</na> the rock into pools of waters, and stony hill into fountains of waters.
<cr>[1]</cr> <sc>Not</sc> TO US <sc>Olord</sc>, <sc>Not</sc> TO US: but to thy name give the glory.
For <na>(i)</na> thy mercy, and <na>(k)</na> thy truth: lest at any time <na>(l)</na> the Gentiles say: Where is their God?
But our God is in heaven: he hath done all things what soever he would.
The idols of the gentiles are silver, and gold, the works of men's hands.
They have mouth, and shall not speak: they have eyes, an shall not see.
They have ears, and shall not hear: they have nostrils and shall not smell.
They have hands, and shall not handle: they have feet, and shall not walk: they shall not cry in their throat.
<na>(m)</na> Let them that make them become like to them: and all that have confidence in them.
<na>(n)</na> The house of Israel hath hoped in our Lord: he is their helper and their protector.
The house of Aaron hath hoped in our Lord: he is their helper and their protector.
They that fear our Lord have hoped in our Lord; he is their helper and their protector.
Our Lord hath been mindful of us: and hath blessed us. He hath blessed the house of Israel: he hath blessed the house of Aaron.
He hath blessed all that fear our Lord, the little with the great.
Our Lord add upon you: upon you, & upon your children.
Blessed be you of our Lord, which made heaven and earth.
The heaven of heaven is to our Lord: but the earth he hath given to the children of men.
The dead shall not praise thee, o Lord: nor all they that go down into hell:
but we that live do bless our Lord, from this time, and for ever.

chapters.summary: For the marvelous passage of Israel out of Aegypt, 3. the red sea, the river of Jordan, 7. and the hills giving them place, 8. the rocks yielding them water, 9. God, not themselves, is to be praised. 12. Idols and Idolaters are vain, and shall be confounded. 17. The faithful trust in God, 20. are blessed, and for ever praise God. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: The marvelous passage of Israel from Aegypt. The 4. key.

verses.text: Alleluia. I <na>(a)</na> have loved, because our Lord <na>(b)</na> will hear the voice of my prayer.
Because he hath inclined his ear to me: and <na>(c)</na> in my days I will invocate.
The sorrows <na>(d)</na> of death have compassed me: and <na>(e)</na> the perils of hell have found me. <na>(f)</na> I have found tribulation and sorrow:
and I <na>(g)</na> invocated the name of our Lord. O Lord deliver my soul:
our Lord is merciful, and just, and our God doth mercy.
Our Lord keepeth little ones: <na>(h)</na> I was humbled, and he hath delivered me.
Turn o my soul into thy rest: because our Lord hath done good to thee.
Because he hath delivered my soul from death: mine eyes from tears, my feet from sliding.
I <na>(i)</na> will please our Lord in the country of the living.

chapters.summary: A just man acknowledging that spiritual life beginneth by faith, and by public profession thereof, 4. gratefully accepteth of Christ's Redemption, 5. dedicateth his life and all he hath to God's service. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: Thanks-giving for our Redeemer. The 5. key.

verses.text: Alleluia. I <na>(a)</na> believed, <na>(b)</na> for which cause I spake: but I <na>(c)</na> was humbled exceedingly. <cr>[1]</cr>
Isaid <na>(d)</na> in mine excess: Every man is a liar.

chapters.summary: Faithful people collected in the Church of Christ, exhort each other to render thanks to God, for their delivery from spiritual and temporal tribulations. 16. The Laity demand participation of Christ's Mysteries, promising to serve him duly: 25. Which the Pastors freely impart, and together with the people, solemnly celebrate God's praise. <na>[1]</na>

<na>(z)</na> Confess ye to our Lord because he is good: because his mercy is for ever. <sc>General</sc> <sc>Annotations</sc> <sc>Upon</sc> <sc>This</sc> <sc>Cxviii</sc>. <sc>Psalm</sc>. As this Psalm is the longest in the whole Psalter, so it seemeth to the ancient Fathers most profound in sense. <cr>[1]</cr> And so much the harder to be understood, because also the very hardness thereof lieth hidden, which in diverse other Psalms and parts of holy Scripture, easily appeareth to the reader. But here the words being clear, and the sense also plain and easy in some points of doctrine, yet the more diligence is employed, the more difficulty is found in searching the whole sense and meaning of every word and sentence, with the manner observed in composing it, and the frequent repetition of the same or like words. All which maturely considered caused that great Clerk, and light of the Church Saint Augustin, <cr>[2]</cr> to omit this Psalm, when he explicated all the rest. And when at last he added also this, he wittingly omitted one special difficulty, which he doubted not to be contained in the manner of composing it, not only by order of the Hebrew Alphabet, as diverse more Psalms, and some other parts of holy Scripture, but more artificially than any other, the first eight verses all beginning with the first letter Aleph; the next eight, with the second letter Beth: and so to the last of the two and twenty letters. Of which omission he yieldeth this only reason, because he found nothing (as he humbly affirmeth) that might properly pertain thereunto. Confessing also expressly, that whensoever he applied his cogitations to expound the text it-self, it always exceeded his hability. But finally to satisfy the often and earnest request of his brethren and friends, trusting (as always) in God's special help, he largely expoundeth it, in thirty two distinct Sermons. <cr>[3]</cr> S. Ambrose, also moved with like piety, <cr>[1]</cr> made two and twenty Sermons in exposition of this Psalm. Affirming in his Prologue, that amongst other Psalms, especially this sheweth how great a master King David was of moral good life. For all Moral doctrine, being of his own nature sweet, yet most delighteth the ears, and gently toucheth the mind, being uttered, as here it is, with pleasantness of verse, and sweetness of song. Again whereas this Royal Prophet in many places of this book poureth out sentences of moral Psalms or songs, <cr>[2]</cr> as bright stars, that shine and glister to all the world, here most excellently he produceth a more singular mirror, as the sun, of full light, burning with meridian heat. And for the profit of all, <cr>[3]</cr> the better to draw our attentions, to learn that we may, though we cannot attain to all that we would, he disposed this Psalm through all the Alphabet: that as children beginning with the first letters, make entrance to further knowledge; so by the same beginnings we should lay the first foundation, and thereupon proceed in our spiritual building, towards perfection in good life, the true service of God. Which is yet further insinuated (as the same Doctor teacheth) by the eight verses continually beginning with the same letter, <cr>[4]</cr> and so other eight in order through the whole Alphabet, to signify that after seven days travail in this temporal life, we may come to that unity, which we expected in the eighth day of resurrection, when we hope to rise revived in our Lord Jesus, in newness of eternal life. Likewise Saint Basil in the Argument of this Psalm admonisheth, <na>◦</na> that whereas holy David, according to diverse states, which he passed, writ diverse Psalms: as when he fled from his enemies, when he lamented his distresses, mourned in pensiveness, enjoyed peace and comfort, ran a right course of virtue, fell from God by sin, & again returning observed God's laws; in this one Psalm he comprehendeth all his prayers made to God at sundry times, & here proposeth the same, a certain profitable moral doctrine, to all sorts and states of men. Neither doth he pretermit doctrinal points of faith, but interposeth them also with moral documents, in such sort, that this one Psalm may suffice to teach the well disposed, how to attain to perfection in virtue, to stir up

Here follow in order fifteen Psalms entitle Gradual Canticles. <cr>[1]</cr> The Hebrew word Mahaloth signifieth Steps, or Ascensions. The reason whereof Aadias, and some other Rabbins yield, for that they were sung with highest elevated notes, that can be in Music. The Talmud saith they are so called, because they were sung in the fifteen steps going up into the Temple. But S. Augustin, S. Basil, and other Christian Fathers expound them according to the history, and immediate prophetical sense, of the delivery of the Jews from captivity of Babylon, <cr>[2]</cr> ascending into Jerusalem: which is so situated on mountains, that the way from all parts was by ascending unto it. According to the Mystical sense, <cr>[3]</cr> of ascending spiritually by virtues to perfection, and to eternal felicity. For the way tending to virtue (saith S. Basil) is like to certain steps (or degrees) by little and little bringing the man that loveth wisdom unto heaven. These Canticles therefore are prayers, mixed with consolations, for the joyful delivery of God's people, from that great captivity in Babylon, which the Psalmist King David saw in prophetical spirit, and which his posterity felt, and sometime endured. Which again as a figure signifieth the return and ascending of mankind from sin to grace, and from the miserable state of this world into heaven. Whereupon S. Augustine interpreteth this prophecy of the ascension, or elevation of the heart, from the vale of tears. <cr>[4]</cr> In the mean time, whiles we are in this world, these Psalms are consolatory prayers, and prophetical assurance, that God's people, Catholic Christians, shall be delivered from the thraldom and commandments are equity.
