chapters.summary: A perpetual recommendation of the singular excellency, absolute necessity, and eternal heavenly profit of God's law: with frequent aspirations to perfection, hatred of sin, love of virtue, and fervent desire to rest in God. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: Perfect justice is in keeping God's law. The 7. key.

verses.text: Agradual Canticle. When I was in tribulation I cried to our Lord: and <na>(a)</na> he heard me.
O Lord deliver my soul from <na>(b)</na> unjust lips, and from a deceitful tongue.
<na>(c)</na> What may be given thee, or what may be added unto thee to a deceitful tongue?
<na>(d)</na> The sharp arrows of the mighty, with coals of desolation.
<na>(e)</na> Woe is to me, that my sojourning is prolonged: I have dwelt with the <na>(f)</na> inhabitants of Cedar:
My soul hath been long a sojourner.
With them that hated peace I was peaceable: when I spake to them, they impugned me <na>(g)</na> without cause.

chapters.summary: The just rejoice that their prayer is heard, and that God continually protecteth them. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: God's protection. The 3. key.

verses.text: Agradual Canticle. I have lifted up mine eyes unto <na>(a)</na> the mountains, <na>(b)</na> from whence help shall come to me.
My help is from our Lord, who made heaven and earth.
Give he not <na>(c)</na> thy foot to be moved: neither do he slumber that keepeth thee.
Lo he shall not slumber nor sleep, that keepeth <na>(d)</na> Israel.
Our Lord keepeth thee, our Lord is thy protection, upon thy right hand.
By day <na>(e)</na> the sun shall not burn thee: nor <na>(f)</na> the moon by night.
Our Lord doth keep thee from all evil: our Lord keep <na>(g)</na> thy soul.
Our Lord keep thy coming in, and thy going out: from hence forth now, and for ever.
Agradual Canticle. I rejoiced in these things, which <na>(a)</na> were said to me: We shall go into the house of our Lord.
<na>(b)</na> Our feet were standing, in thy courts, o Jerusalem.
Jerusalem, which is built as a city: whose <na>(c)</na> participation is together in it-self.
For <na>(d)</na> thither did the tribes ascend, <na>(e)</na> the tribes of our Lord: the testimony of Israel to confess unto the name of our Lord.
Because seats <na>(f)</na> sat there in judgment, <na>(g)</na> seats upon the house <na>(h)</na> of David.
<na>(i)</na> Ask ye the things that are for the peace of Jerusalem: and <na>(k)</na> aboundance to them that love thee.
Peace be made in thy strength: and aboundance in thy towers.
<na>(l)</na> For my brethren, and my neighbours' sakes, <na>(m)</na> I spake peace of thee:
For the house of our Lord God, I have sought <na>(n)</na> good things to thee.

chapters.summary: A fervent and attentive prayer, to be delivered from captivity, or any other affliction. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: A prayer in affliction. The 7. key.

verses.text: Agradual Canticle. To thee have I lifted up mine eyes, which dwellest in the heavens.

Behold as the eyes <na>(a)</na> of servants, are on the hands of their masters, As the eyes <na>(b)</na> of the handmaid on the hands of her mistress: so are our eyes to our Lord God until he have mercy on us.
Have mercy on us, o Lord, have mercy on us: because we are <na>(c)</na> much replenished with contempt.
Because our soul is <na>(d)</na> much replenished: reproach to them that abound, and contempt to the proud.
Agradual Canticle. But that our Lord <na>(a)</na> was in us, let Israel now say,
but that our Lord was in us. <na>(b)</na> When men rose up against us,
<na>(c)</na> perhaps they had swallowed us <na>(d)</na> alive: When their fury was angry against us,
perhaps <na>(e)</na> water had swallowed us.
Our soul hath passed through <na>(f)</na> a torrent: perhaps our soul had passed through an intolerable water.
Blessed be our Lord which hath not given us for a prey to their teeth.
Our soul as a sparrow is delivered from the snare of the fowlers: The snare is <na>(g)</na> broken, and we are delivered.
Our help is in the name of our Lord, who made heaven and earth.

chapters.summary: The Church, and holy members thereof, are always protected by God: 5. The wicked being suffered for a while, are at last punished. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: God's protection. The 3. key.

verses.text: Agradual Canticle. They <na>(a)</na> that trust in our Lord, as mount Sion: he shall not be moved for ever <na>(b)</na> that dwelleth
in Jerusalem. Mountains round about it: and our Lord round about his people, from hence forth now and for ever.
Because our Lord <na>(c)</na> will not leave the rod of sinners upon the lot of the just: that the just reach not their hands to iniquity.
<na>(d)</na> Do well, o Lord, to the good, and right of heart.
But <na>(e)</na> those that decline into obligations, our Lord will bring with them that work iniquity: <na>(f)</na> peace upon Israel.
Agradual Canticle. When our Lord turned the captivity of Sion: we were made as <na>(a)</na> men comforted.
Then was our mouth replenished with joy: and our tongue with exultation. Then <na>(b)</na> shall they say among the Gentiles: Our Lord hath done magnifically with them.
<na>(c)</na> Our Lord hath done magnifically with us: we are made joyful.
<na>(d)</na> Turn our captivity, o Lord, <na>(e)</na> as a torrent in the South.
<na>(f)</na> They that sow in tears, <na>(g)</na> shall reap in joyfulness.
Going they went and wept, casting their seeds. But coming they shall come with exultation, carrying their sheaves.

chapters.summary: Neither house nor city can be built, or kept without God's special providence and help, 3. those that trust in him shall prosper in their endeavours. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: God's help in all good works. The 3. key.

verses.text: Agradual Canticle <na>(a)</na> of Salomon. Unless <na>(b)</na> our Lord build the house, they have laboured in vain that build it. Unless our Lord keep the city, he watcheth in vain that keepeth it.
It is vain for you to rise <na>(c)</na> before light: <na>(d)</na> rise ye after ye have sitten, which eat <na>(e)</na> the bread of sorrow.
<na>(f)</na> When he shall give <na>(g)</na> sleep to his beloved: behold <na>(h)</na> the inheritance of our Lord <na>(i)</na> are children: <na>(k)</na> the reward, the fruit of the womb.
As <na>(l)</na> arrows in the hand of the mighty: so are the <na>(m)</na> children of them that are shaken.
<na>(n)</na> Blessed is the man that hath filled his desire of them: he shall <na>(o)</na> not be confounded when he shall speak to his enemies <na>(p)</na> in the gate.
Agradual Canticle. Blessed are all that fear our Lord, that walk in his ways.
Because thou shalt eat the labours of thy hands: blessed art thou, and it shall be well with thee.
Thy <na>(a)</na> wife as a fruitful vine, in <na>(b)</na> the sides of thy house. <na>(c)</na> Thy children as young plants of olive-trees, round about thy table.
Behold so shall the man be blessed, that feareth our Lord.
Our Lord out of Sion bless thee: and that thou mayst see the good things of Jerusalem all the days of thy life.
And that thou mayst see thy <na>(d)</na> children's children, peace upon Israel.

chapters.summary: The Church often (3. and much) impugned, is not overcome. 4. Her persecuters are destroyed, 6. contemned, 8. and cursed. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: The Church still firm in persecution. The 6. key.

verses.text: Agradual Canticle. <na>(a)</na> Often have they impugned me <na>(b)</na> from my youth, let Israel now say:
Often have they impugned me from my youth: <na>(c)</na> but they have not prevailed against me.
Sinners <na>(d)</na> have builded upon my back: they have <na>(e)</na> prolonged their iniquity.
<na>(f)</na> Our just Lord will cut the necks of sinners:
let them all <na>(g)</na> be confounded and <na>(h)</na> turned backward, that hate Sion.
Let them be made as grass in the tops of houses: which is withered before it be plucked up.
Whereof the reaper hath not filled his hand, and he that gathereth the sheaves his bosom.
And they said not that passed by: The blessing of our Lord be upon you: we have blessed you in the name of our Lord.
Agradual Canticle. From <na>(a)</na> the depths I have cried to thee, o Lord:
Lord hear my voice: Let thine ears be intent to the voice of my petition.
If thou shalt observe iniquities, o Lord: Lord <na>(b)</na> who shall sustain it?
Because with thee there is <na>(c)</na> propitiation: and for <na>(d)</na> thy law I have expected thee, o Lord. My soul hath expected in his word:
my soul hath hoped in our Lord.
From <na>(e)</na> the morning watch even until night: let Israel hope in our Lord.
Because with our Lord there is mercy: and with him <na>(f)</na> plenteous redemption.
And he shall redeem <na>(g)</na> Israel, from all his iniquities.

chapters.summary: The Jews, or other people in tribulation for sin, or temporal captivity, cry to God to be delivered: 4. trusting and encouraging each other in God's accustomed mercy, assuredly hoping that he will redeem & deliver them. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: The sixth penitential Psalm. The 7. key.

verses.text: Agradual Canticle of <na>(a)</na> David. Lord my heart is not exalted: <na>(b)</na> neither are mine eyes lofty. Neither have I walked in great matters: nor in marvelous things above me.
If I was not humbly minded: but exalted my soul: As <na>(c)</na> the weaned child is toward his mother, so <na>(d)</na> retribution in my soul.
<na>(e)</na> Let Israel hope in our Lord, from henceforth now and for ever.

chapters.summary: The Psalmist earnestly prayeth God to give him leave, & to shew him where to build a Temple. But more especially prayeth for, and prophesieth the coming of Christ, the promised Son of David: 14. signifying God's promise thereof, and of establishing his Church. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: Christ's coming to restore man. The 5. key.

verses.text: Agradual Canticle. Remember <na>(a)</na> David, o Lord, and all his meekness:
As he sware to our Lord, <na>(b)</na> vowed a vow to the God of Jacob.
<na>(c)</na> If I shall enter into the tabernacle of my house, if I shall ascend into the bed of my couch.
If I shall give sleep to mine eyes, and slumbering to mine eye lids:
And rest to my temples: until I find a place for our Lord, a tabernacle for the God of Jacob.
Behold we have heard of it <na>(d)</na> in Ephrata: we have found it in <na>(e)</na> the fields of the wood.
<na>(f)</na> We will enter into his tabernacle: we will <na>(g)</na> adore in the place where his feet stood.
<na>(h)</na> Arise Lord into thy rest, thou, and <na>(i)</na> the ark of thy sanctification.
Let thy Priests be clothed with <na>(k)</na> justice: & let thy <na>(l)</na> saints rejoice.
<na>(m)</na> For David thy servant's sake, <na>(n)</na> turn not away the face of thy Christ.
Our Lord <na>(o)</na> hath sworn truth to David, and <na>(p)</na> he will not disappoint it: <na>(q)</na> Of the fruit of thy womb I will set upon thy seat.
<na>(r)</na> If thy children shall keep my testament, and these my testimonies which I will teach them: Their children also even for ever, shall sit upon thy seat.
Because our Lord hath chosen Sion: he hath chosen it for an habitation to himself.
This is my <na>(s)</na> rest for ever and ever: here will I dwell because I have chosen it.
Blessing I will bless her <na>(t)</na> widow: her <na>(v)</na> poor I will fill with breads.
Her <na>(w)</na> Priests I will clothe with salvation: and her <na>(x)</na> saints shall rejoice with joyfulness.
<na>(y)</na> Thither will I bring forth a horn to David, I have prepared <na>(z)</na> a lamp to my Christ.
His enemies I will clothe with confusion: but upon him shall my sanctification flourish.
Agradual Canticle <na>(a)</na> of David. Behold <na>(b)</na> how good and how pleasant a thing it is, for brethren to dwell in one.
<na>(c)</na> As ointment on <na>(d)</na> the head, which ran down upon <na>(e)</na> the beard, the beard of Aaron, which ran down unto <na>(f)</na> the hem of his garment.
<na>(g)</na> As the dew of Hermon, which runneth down upon mount Sion. Because <na>(h)</na> there hath our Lord commanded blessing, and life even for ever.

chapters.summary: Fraternal concord is commended to all in the Church, as necessary and delectable in itself, and blessed of God. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: Fraternal concord. The 7. key.

verses.text: Agradual Canticle. Lo <na>(a)</na> now bless our Lord, all ye the servants of our Lord: Which stand in the house of our Lord, in the courts of the house of our God.
In the nights lift up your hands unto the holy places, and bless ye our Lord.
Our Lord out of Sion bless thee, who <na>(b)</na> made heaven and earth.

chapters.summary: God who only is omnipotent, and fountain of all goodness, is of all to be praised. 8. He is the special protector of his elected people. 15. Contrarywise false gods are impotent, vain, and cannot help those that serve them. 19. Only the Church doth rightly praise him. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: God's perfection and goodness. The 1. key.

verses.text: Alleluia. Praise ye the name of our Lord, <na>(a)</na> ye servants praise our Lord.
Ye <na>(b)</na> that stand in the house of our Lord, in <na>(c)</na> the courts of the house of our God.
Praise ye our Lord, because our Lord is <na>(d)</na> good: sing ye to his name, because it is <na>(e)</na> sweet.
Because our Lord <na>(f)</na> hath chosen Jacob to himself, Israel for his own possession.
Because I have known that our Lord is <na>(g)</na> great, and our God above all gods.
All things whatsoever our Lord would he hath done, in heaven, in earth, in the sea, and in all the depths.
Bring forth clouds from the uttermost of the earth: lightnings he hath turned into rain. Who bringeth forth the winds out of his treasures:
who struck the first begotten of Aegypt from man even to beast.
He sent forth signs and wonders in the midst of thee, o Aegypt: upon Pharao, and upon all his servants. <cr>[1]</cr>
Who struck many nations: and slew strong kings:
Sehon the king of the Amorrheites, & Og the king of Basan, and all the Kingdoms of Chanaan. <cr>[1]</cr>
And he gave their land for inheritance, for an inheritance to Israel his people.
Lord thy name is for ever: Lord thy memorial is unto generation and generation.
Because our Lord shall judge his people: and will be entreated toward his servants.
The idols of the Gentiles are silver, and gold, the works of men's hands.
They have mouth, & shall not speak: they have eyes, and shall not see.
They have ears, and shall not hear: for neither is there breath in their mouth.
Let them that make them become like to them: and all that have confidence in them.
Ye house of Israel bless our Lord: ye house of Aaron bless our Lord.
Ye house of Levi bless our Lord: you that fear our Lord, bless ye our Lord.
Blessed be our Lord out of Sion, who dwelleth in Jerusalem.
Alleluia. Confess ye to our Lord <na>(a)</na> because he is good: <na>(b)</na> because his mercy is for ever.
Confess ye to <na>(c)</na> the God of gods: because his mercy is for ever.
<na>(d)</na> Confess ye to <na>(e)</na> the Lord of lords: because his mercy is for ever
Who <na>(f)</na> only doth great marvels: because his mercy is for ever.
Who made the heaven <na>(g)</na> in understanding: because his mercy is for ever.
Who established <na>(h)</na> the earth over the waters: because his mercy is for ever.
Who made the great lights: because his mercy is for ever.
The sun to rule the day: because his mercy is for ever.
The moon and stars to rule the night: because his mercy is for ever.
Who struck Aegypt with their first-begotten: because his mercy is for ever. <cr>[1]</cr>
Who brought forth Israel out of the midst of them: because his mercy is for ever.
In a mighty hand, and lofty arm: because his mercy is for ever. <cr>[1]</cr>
Who divided the Red sea into divisions: because his mercy is for ever.
And brought forth Israel through the midst thereof: because his mercy is for ever.
And he overthrew Pharao, and his host in the Red sea: because his mercy is for ever. <cr>[1]</cr>
Who led his people through the desert: because his mercy is for ever. <cr>[1]</cr>
Who struck great kings: because his mercy is for ever.
And slew strong kings: because his mercy is for ever.
Sehon the king of the Amorrheites: because his mercy is for ever.
And Og the king of Basan: because his mercy is for ever:
And he gave their land for an inheritance: because his mercy is for ever.
For an inheritance to Israel his servant: because his mercy is for ever.
For in our humiliation he was mindful of us: because his mercy is for ever.
And he redeemed us from our enemies: because his mercy is for ever.
Who giveth food to <na>(i)</na> all flesh: because his mercy is for ever.
Confess ye to the God of heaven: because his mercy is for ever. Confess ye to the Lord of lords: because his mercy is for ever.

chapters.summary: God being marvelous in himself, 4. hath shewed his power and goodness in his works, as well in general to all the world, 10. as in particular towards his elected people. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: God's marvelous works. The 2. key.

verses.text: A Psalm of David <na>(a)</na> for Jeremy. Upon the rivers <na>(b)</na> of Babylon, there we <na>(c)</na> sat and wept: whilst we <na>(d)</na> remembered Sion.
On the willows in the midst thereof, we hanged up <na>(e)</na> our instruments.
Because there they that led us captive, <na>(f)</na> demanded of us words of songs. And they that led us away: Sing ye an hymn to us of the songs of Sion.
<na>(g)</na> How shall we sing the song of our Lord in a strange land?
<na>(h)</na> If I shall forget thee, o Jerusalem, let my right hand be forgotten.
<na>(i)</na> Let my tongue cleave to my jaws, if I do not remember thee: If I shall not set Jerusalem in the beginning of my joy.
Be mindful, o Lord, of <na>(k)</na> the children of Edom, in <na>(l)</na> the day of Jerusalem: <na>(m)</na> That say: Rase it, rase it, even unto the foundation thereof.
<na>(n)</na> Daughter of Babylon miserable: blessed is he, that shall repay thee thy payment, which thou hast payed us.
Blessed is he <na>(o)</na> that shall hold, and <na>(p)</na> shall dash thy little ones against the rock.

chapters.summary: The Prophet describeth how lamentably the people in captivity of Babylon will bewail the want of means to serve God, and of their native soil: 7. with just desire of their enemies' punishment. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: The Jews' lamentation in captivity. The 4. key.

verses.text: To <na>(a)</na> David himself. I will <na>(b)</na> confess to thee, o Lord, in my whole heart: <na>(c)</na> because thou hast heard the words of my mouth. In <na>(d)</na> the sight of Angels I will sing to thee:
Iwill adore <na>(e)</na> toward thy holy temple, and will confess to thy name: <cr>[1]</cr> For thy mercy, and thy truth: because thou hast magnified above every thing <na>(f)</na> thy holy name.
In what day soever I shall invocate thee, hear me: thou wilt multiply strength in my soul.
Let <na>(g)</na> all the kings of the earth, o Lord, confess to thee: because they have heard all the words of thy mouth:
And let them sing in the ways of our Lord: because great is the glory of our Lord.
Because our Lord is high, and he beholdeth low things: and high things he knoweth <na>(h)</na> far off.
If I shall walk in the midst of tribulation, thou wilt quicken me: and upon the wrath of mine enemies thou hast extended thy hand, and thy right hand hath saved me.
Our Lord will repay for me: o Lord thy mercy is for ever: despise not the works of thy hands.

chapters.summary: God's knowledge, 7. and presence (10. without the help, or hinderance of any thing) extendeth to all things, times, and places. 17. He giveth exceeding great honour to his saints, 20. the wicked, as enemies of God, are justly hated, 23. the just pray for God's perpetual direction. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: God's special providence of his servants. The 3. key.

verses.text: Unto <na>(a)</na> the end, a Psalm of David. Lord thou <na>(b)</na> hast proved me, and hast known me:
thou hast known my sitting down, and my rising up.
Thou hast understood my cogitations far off: my path, and <na>(c)</na> my cord thou hast searched out.
And thou hast foreseen all my ways: because there is not a word in my <na>(d)</na> tongue.
Behold, o Lord, thou hast known all the last things, & them of old: thou hast formed me, and hast put thy hand upon me.
Thy knowledge is <na>(e)</na> become marvelous of me: it is made great, and I cannot reach to it.
<na>(f)</na> Whither shall I go from thy spirit? And whither shall I fly from thy face?
If I shall ascend into heaven, thou art there: if I descend into hell, thou art present.
If I shall take my wings early, and dwell in the extreme parts of the sea.
Certes thither also shall thy hand conduct me: and thy right hand shall hold me.
<na>(g)</na> And I said: Perhaps darkness shall tread over me: and the night is mine illumination in my delights.
For darkness shall not be darkened from thee, and the night shall be lightened as the day: as the darkness thereof, so also the light thereof.
Because thou hast possessed <na>(h)</na> my reins: thou hast received me from <na>(i)</na> my mother's womb.
Iwill confess to thee, because thou art terribly magnified: thy works are marvelous, & my soul knoweth exceedingly.
My <na>(k)</na> bone is not hid from thee, which thou madest in secret: and my substance in the lower parts of the earth.
<na>(l)</na> Mine <cr>[1]</cr> imperfection thine eyes have seen, & in thy book <cr>[2]</cr> all shall be written: <na>(m)</na> days shall be formed, & no man in them.
<na>(n)</na> But to me thy <cr>[1]</cr> friends, o God, are become honourable exceedingly: their principality is exceedingly strengthened.
<na>(o)</na> I will number them, and they shall be multiplied above the sand: <na>(p)</na> I rose up and I am yet with thee.
<na>(q)</na> If thou shalt kill sinners, o God: <na>(r)</na> ye men of blood depart from me.
<na>(s)</na> Because you say in thought: they <na>(t)</na> shall receive thy cities in vain.
<na>(v)</na> Did not I hate them that hate thee, o Lord: and <na>(w)</na> pined away because of thine enemies?
<na>(x)</na> with perfect hatred did I hate them: they are become <na>(y)</na> enemies to me.
<na>(z)</na> Prove me, o God, and know my heart: examine me, and know my paths.
And <na>(a)</na> see, if the way of iniquity be in me: <na>(b)</na> and conduct me in the everlasting way.
Unto the end, a Psalm of David.
Deliver me, o Lord, from the evil man: from the unjust man rescue me.
Which <na>(a)</na> have devised iniquity in their heart: <na>(b)</na> all the day they did appoint battles. They have whet their tongues as that of a serpent:
the venom of asps is under their lips.
Keep me, o Lord, from the hand of the sinner: and from unjust men deliver me.
Who have devised to supplant my steps: the proud have hid a snare for me: And they have stretched out ropes for a snare: they have laid <cr>[1]</cr> a stumbling block for me near the way.
<na>(c)</na> I said to our Lord: Thou art my God: hear, o Lord, the voice of my petition.
O Lord, Lord <na>(d)</na> the strength of my salvation: <na>(e)</na> thou hast overshadowed my head in the day of battle.
Yield me not, o Lord, from <na>(f)</na> my desire to the sinner: they have devised against me, forsake me not, <na>(g)</na> lest they perhaps be proud.
<na>(h)</na> The head of their compass: <na>(i)</na> the labour of their lips <na>(k)</na> shall cover them.
<na>(l)</na> Coals shall fall upon them, <na>(m)</na> thou shalt cast them down into fire: in miseries <na>(n)</na> they shall not stand up.
Aman full of tongue shall not be directed in the earth: evils shall take the unjust man into destruction.
<na>(o)</na> I have known that our Lord will do the judgment of the needy: and the revenge of the poor.
But as for the just, they shall confess to thy name: and the righteous shall dwell with <na>(p)</na> thy countenance.

chapters.summary: The just diversly afflicted by the wicked, pray to be defended, 7. repose their confidence in God. 10. Who will adjudge the reprobate to eternal punishment, 13. and reward the good with the fruition of himself. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: Eternal pain of the wicked, and joy of the blessed. The 10. key.

verses.text: A Psalm of David. Lord I have cried to thee, hear me: attend to my voice when I shall cry to thee.
<cr>[1]</cr> Let my prayer be directed as <na>(a)</na> incense in thy sight: the elevation of my hands as <na>(b)</na> evening sacrifice.
Set o Lord <na>(c)</na> a watch to my mouth: and <na>(d)</na> a door round about to my lips.
Decline not my heart <na>(e)</na> into words of malice, to make excuses in sins. With men that work iniquity: and I will not communicate with the chief of them.

The just shall rebuke me in mercy, and <na>(f)</na> shall reprehend me: but let not the oil of a sinner <na>(g)</na> fat my head. Because <na>(h)</na> yet also my prayer is in their <na>(i)</na> good pleasures:
<na>(k)</na> their judges are swallowed up joined to the rock. <na>(l)</na> They shall hear my words <na>(m)</na> because they have prevailed:
<na>(n)</na> as the grossness of the earth is broken out upon the earth. Our <na>(o)</na> bones are dissipated <na>(p)</na> near to hell:
<na>(q)</na> for to thee, o Lord, Lord are mine eyes; in thee have I hoped, take not away my <cr>[1]</cr> soul.
Keep me from <na>(r)</na> the snare, which they have set for me: and from <na>(s)</na> the scandals of them that work iniquity.
<na>(t)</na> Sinners shall fall in his net: <na>(v)</na> I am alone until I pass.

chapters.summary: The Church prayeth that her children may avoid sinful words, 4. not make excuses of sins committed, not communicate with others in sin, nor to hearken to flatterers: 6. but to pray that they may amend (the Psalmist by the way prophesieth that many shall be converted.) 8. Though sometimes persecution be great, the Church faileth not. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: The Church prayeth and prevaileth. The 6. key.

verses.text: Of <na>(a)</na> understanding to David, when he was in the cave, a prayer. <cr>[1]</cr>
With my voice I <na>(b)</na> have cried to our Lord: with my voice I have prayed to our Lord:
Ipour out my prayer in his sight, and I pronounce my tribulation before him.
<na>(c)</na> When my spirit faileth of myself, and <na>(d)</na> thou hast known my paths. In this way, which I walked, they hid a snare for me.
Ilooked toward <na>(e)</na> the right hand, and <na>(f)</na> saw: and <na>(g)</na> there was none that would know me. <na>(h)</na> Flight hath failed me: and there is none to require my soul.
<na>(i)</na> I have cried to thee, o Lord, I have said: Thou art <na>(k)</na> my hope, <na>(l)</na> my portion <na>(m)</na> in the land of the living.
Attend to my petition: because I am <na>(n)</na> humbled exceedingly. Deliver me from them that persecute me: because they are made strong over me.
Bring forth my soul out of prison, <na>(o)</na> to confess unto thy name: <na>(p)</na> the just expect me, <na>(q)</na> till thou reward me.

chapters.summary: King David (or any other) in spiritual or temporal tribulation, not trusting in his own justice, layeth open his calamity, 5. considering God's benignity, 6. prayeth

verses.text: to be speedily delivered; 11. and confidently assureth himself thereof. [1] [1] The seventh penitential Psalm. The 7. key.
A Psalm of David, when Absalom his son persecuted him. <cr>[1]</cr> Lord hear my prayer: with thine ears receive my petition in <na>(a)</na> thy truth: hear me in <na>(b)</na> thy justice.
And <na>(c)</na> enter not into judgment with thy servant: <na>(d)</na> because no man living shall be justified in thy sight.
Because the enemy <na>(e)</na> hath persecuted my soul: he <na>(f)</na> hath humbled my life in the earth. <na>(g)</na> He hath set me in obscure places as the dead of the world:
and my spirit is in anguish upon me, within me my heart is troubled.
<na>(h)</na> I was mindful of old days, I have meditated in all thy works: in the facts of thy hands did I meditate.
Ihave <na>(i)</na> stretched forth my hands to thee: my soul is <na>(k)</na> as earth without water unto thee.
Hear me <na>(l)</na> quickly, o Lord: my spirit hath fainted. Turn not away thy face from me: <na>(m)</na> and I shall be like to them that descend into <na>(n)</na> the lake.
Make me hear thy mercy <na>(o)</na> in the morning: because I have hoped in thee. Make the way known to me, wherein I may walk: <na>(p)</na> because I have lifted up my soul to thee.
Deliver me from mine enemies, o Lord, to thee I have fled:
teach me to do thy will, because thou art my God. Thy <na>(q)</na> good spirit will conduct me into the right way:
for thy name sake, o Lord, thou wilt quicken me in thine equity. Thou wilt bring forth my soul out of tribulation:
and in thy mercy thou wilt destroy mine enemies. And thou wilt destroy all that afflict my soul: because I am thy servant.
A Psalm of David, <na>(a)</na> against Goliath. Blessed be our Lord my God, who <na>(b)</na> teacheth my hands to battle, and my fingers to war.
My mercy, and my refuge: my defender, and my deliverer. My protector; and I have hoped in him, who <na>(c)</na> subdueth my people under me.
Lord <na>(d)</na> what is man, that thou art <na>(e)</na> made known to him? Or the son of man, <na>(f)</na> that thou esteemest him?
<na>(g)</na> Man is made like to vanity: his days pass as a shadow.
<na>(h)</na> Lord incline thy heavens, and descend: touch the mountains, and they will smoke.
Lighten lightning, and thou shalt disperse them: shoot out thine arrows, and thou shalt destroy them.
Send forth thy hand from on high, take me out, and deliver me from many <na>(i)</na> waters: from the hand <na>(k)</na> of children <na>(l)</na> strangers.
Whose mouth hath spoken <na>(m)</na> vanity: and their right hand is the right hand of iniquity.
O God I will sing to thee a new song: in <na>(n)</na> the psalter of ten strings, I will sing to thee.
Who givest salvation to kings: who hast redeemed David thy servant from the malignant sword:
deliver me. And rescue me out of the hand of <na>(o)</na> children strangers, whose mouth hath spoken vanity: and their right hand, is the right hand of iniquity.
Whose sons, are as new plants in their youth. Their daughters comely trimmed: decked about after the similitude of a temple.
Their store-houses full, flowing out of this into that. Their ewes full of young, abounding in their going forth:
their oxen are fat. There is no ruin of wall, nor <cr>[1]</cr> passage, nor cry in their streets.
They have said, that it is a happy people, which hath these things: <na>(p)</na> blessed is the people, whose <na>(q)</na> God is our Lord.

chapters.summary: The royal Prophet thanketh God for all his victories, & possession of the Kingdom. 3. Admiring God's benignity towards man, 5. prayeth to be still defended from all enemies, 9. promiseth a new song of praise, 11. describeth the vanity of worldly men, 15. concluding that true felicity is in serving God. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: King David praiseth God for his victories. The 8. key.

verses.text: Praising, <na>(a)</na> to David himself. I will exalt thee my God <na>(b)</na> the King: and I will bless thy name <na>(c)</na> for ever and <na>(d)</na> for ever.
Every day will I bless thee: and will praise thy name for ever, and for ever and ever.
Great is our Lord and exceeding laudable, and of his greatness there is no end.
Generation and generation shall praise thy works: and they shall pronounce thy power.
They shall speak the magnificence of the glory of thy holiness: and shall tell thy marvelous works.
And they shall tell the force of thy terrible things: and shall declare thy greatness:
They shall utter the memory of the aboundance of thy sweetness: and in thy justice they shall rejoice.
Our Lord is pitiful and merciful: patient and very merciful.
Our Lord is sweet to all: and his commiserations are over all his works.
Let all thy works, o Lord, confess to thee: and let thy Saints bless thee.
They shall tell the glory of thy kingdom: and shall speak thy might.
That they may make thy might known to the children of men: and the glory of the magnificence of thy kingdom.
Thy kingdom is a kingdom of all worlds: and thy dominion in all generation and generation. Our Lord is faithful in all his words, and holy in all his works.
Our Lord lifteth up all that fall: and setteth up all that are bruised.
The eyes of all hope in thee, o Lord: and thou givest their meat in time convenient.
Thou openest thy hand: and fillest every living creature with blessing.
Our Lord is just in all his ways: and holy in all his works.
Our Lord is near to all that invocate him: to all that invocate him in truth.
He will do the will of them that fear him, and will hear their prayer, and save them.
Our Lord keepeth all that love him: and he will destroy all sinners.
My mouth shall speak the praise of our Lord: and let all flesh bless his holy name for ever, and for ever and ever.

chapters.summary: God is, and for ever ought to be praised, 3. for his immensive, infinite, glorious Majesty, marvelous works, merciful benefits; for his power, wisdom, justice, 19. who will reward the good, and destroy the wicked. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: God's Majesty excelleth all things. The 1. key.

verses.text: Alleluia, <na>(a)</na> of Aggaeus and Zacharie.
My soul praise thou our Lord, I will praise our Lord in my life: I will sing to my God as long as I shall be. Put not confidence in Princes:
in <na>(b)</na> the sons of men: in whom there is no salvation.
His spirit shall go forth, and he shall return into <na>(c)</na> his earth: in that day all their cogitations shall perish.
Blessed is he, whose helper is the God of Jacob, his hope in our Lord his God:
who made heaven and earth, the sea, and all things that are in them.
Which keepeth truth for ever, doth judgment for them that suffer wrong: giveth food to the hungry. Our Lord looseth the fettered:
our Lord illuminateth the blind. Our Lord lifteth up the bruised, our Lord loveth the just.
Our Lord keepeth strangers, the pupil and widow he will receive: and the ways of sinners he will destroy.
Our Lord will reign for ever^ thy God, o Sion in generation & generation.

chapters.summary: God is also to be praised by his peculiar people, for particular benefits, 4. & for his omnipotent power, wisdom, goodness, in creating and governing this whole world, 11. and most special benignity towards those that trust in him. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: God's excellency in creating and governing the world. The 2. key.

verses.text: Alleluia. Praise ye our Lord because Psalm <na>(a)</na> is good: to our God let there be pleasant and comely praise.
Our Lord building up Jerusalem: <na>(b)</na> will gather together the dispersions of Israel.
Who <na>(c)</na> healeth the contrite of heart: and bindeth up their sores.
Who <na>(d)</na> numbereth the multitude of stars: & <na>(e)</na> giveth names to them all.
Great is our Lord, and great is his strength: and of his wisdom there is no <na>(f)</na> number.
Our Lord receiving the meek: & humbling sinners even to the ground.
Sing ye to our Lord in confession: sing ye to our God on harp.
Who <na>(g)</na> covereth the heaven with clouds: and prepareth rain for the earth. Who bringeth forth grass in the mountains: and herb for the service of men.
Who giveth to beasts their food: and to <na>(h)</na> the young ravens that call upon him.
He shall not have pleasure in the strength of an horse: nor in the legs of a man shall he be well pleased.
Our Lord is well pleased toward them that fear him: and in them that hope upon his mercy.
Alleluia. O <na>(a)</na> Jerusalem praise our Lord: praise thy God, o Sion. <cr>[1]</cr>
Because he <na>(b)</na> hath strengthened the locks of thy gates: he <na>(c)</na> hath blessed thy children in thee.
Who <na>(d)</na> hath set thy borders peace: and filleth thee with <na>(e)</na> the fat of corn.
Who sendeth forth <na>(f)</na> his speech to the earth: his word <na>(g)</na> runneth swiftly.
Who giveth <na>(h)</na> snow as wool: scattereth <na>(i)</na> mist as ashes.
He casteth <na>(k)</na> his crystal as morsels: before the face of his cold <na>(l)</na> who shall endure?
He <na>(m)</na> shall send forth his word, and shall melt them: <na>(n)</na> his spirit shall blow, and <na>(o)</na> waters shall flow.
Who declareth his word to <na>(p)</na> Jacob: his justices and judgments to Israel.
He hath <na>(q)</na> not done in like manner to any nation: and his judgements he hath not made manifest to them. <na>(r)</na> Alleluia.

chapters.summary: Again God is to be praised for his goodness towards his peculiar people, 4. yea towards all the world: but most aboundantly towards his Church. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: God's providence especially towards the Church. The 6. key.

verses.text: Alleluia. Praise ye our Lord <na>(a)</na> from the heavens: praise ye him in the high places.
Praise ye him all his Angels: praise ye him <na>(b)</na> all his hosts:
Praise ye him <na>(c)</na> sun and moon: praise him all ye stars, and light.
Praise him ye heavens of heavens: and the waters that are above the heavens,
let them praise the name of our Lord. Because he said, and they were made: he commanded, and they were created.
He established them for ever, and for ever and ever: he put a precept, and it shall not pass.
Praise our Lord from the earth: ye dragons, and all depths.
Fire, hail, snow, ice, spirit of storms: which do his word:
Mountains, and all little hills: trees that bear fruit, and all cedars.
Beasts, and all cattle: serpents, and feathered fowls:
<na>(d)</na> Kings of the earth, & all peoples: Princes, and all judges of the earth.
Young men and virgins: old with young let them praise the name of our Lord:
because the name of him <na>(e)</na> alone is exalted.

The confession of him above heaven and earth: and he hath exalted <na>(f)</na> the horn of his people. An hymn to all his <na>(g)</na> saints: to the children of Israel a people <na>(h)</na> approaching unto him. <na>(i)</na> Alleluia.

chapters.summary: All creatures spiritual and corporal, are invited to praise God, their Creator and Conserver, 13. as incomparably excellent. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: Our Creator to be praised by all creatures. The 2. key.

verses.text: Alleluia. Sing ye to our Lord a new song: let his praise be in the <na>(a)</na> Church of saints.
Let Israel be joyful in him, that made him: and let the children of Sion rejoice in their King.
Let them praise his name in choir: on timbrel and psalter let them sing to him.
Because our Lord is well pleased in his people: and he will exalt the meek unto salvation.
The saints shall rejoice in glory: they shall be joyful in <na>(b)</na> their beds.
The <na>(c)</na> exaltations of God in their throat: and <na>(d)</na> two edged swords in their hands.
To do revenge in the nations: chastisements among the peoples.
To bind their kings in fetters: and their nobles in iron manacles.
That they may do in them the judgement that is <na>(e)</na> written: <na>(f)</na> this glory is to all his saints. Alleluia.

chapters.summary: God absolutely most excellent is to be praised, 3. with all sorts of instruments, and by all other means. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: God most excellent and most laudable. The first key.

verses.text: Alleluia. Praise ye our Lord <na>(a)</na> in his holies: praise ye him in <na>(b)</na> the firmament of his strength.
Praise ye him in <na>(c)</na> his powers: praise ye him <na>(d)</na> according to the multitude of his greatness.
<na>(e)</na> Praise ye him <na>(f)</na> in the sound of trumpet: praise ye him on psalter and harp.

Praise ye him on timbrel and (g) choir: praise ye him on (h) strings and organ. S. Augustin in the conclusion of his Enarrations or Sermons upon the Psalms, explicateth a mystery in the number of an hundred and fifty, signifying the concord of the two Testaments. ◦ For in the old testament they kept the Sabbath, which is the seventh day: in the new we keep our Lord's day, after the sabbath, that is, the eighth: which seven and eight (making fifteen) multiplied by ten, signifying the Law of ten commandments, rise unto 150. Again, seven multiplied by seven make 49. whereto one (to wit the eight) being added make fifty, which multiplied by three [1] , signifying the B. Trinity, make 150. Neither seemed it without cause to this great Doctor, that the first fifty end with a Psalm of Penance, craving mercy & remission of sins: the second with Mercy & Justice, which God joineth in the Redemption, Justification, and Salvation of men: the last with Divine Praises, signifying, that by condemning sins in ourselves, through God's mercy we may be justified, and so begin in this life, which is to be perfected in the next, to praise our Lord, as S. Paul admonisheth with Psalms, Hymns, and Spiritual Songs. Concluding with the two verses, appointed by S. Damasus Pope, to be added in the end of all Psalms, and is observed ever since his time by tradition in the whole Church. [2] Glory to the Father, and to the Son, and to the Holy Ghost: As it was in the beginning, and now, and ever, into worlds of worlds. (in eternity without end.) Amen. ◦
Praise ye him on well sounding cymbals: praise ye him on cymbals of jubilation:
let every <na>(i)</na> spirit praise our Lord. Alleluia.
